## 2026-06-18 - v0.23a CleanerSmartisan APK-Only Binary Locale Prune

Goal:

```text
Continue the English/Chinese-only hard-prune route without cleaning disk space
because local free space is still above the 20 GiB threshold. Convert the next
reviewed P1 package, CleanerSmartisan, into APK-only evidence.
```

Preflight and source review:

```text
package:
  com.smartisanos.cleaner
stock path:
  /system/app/CleanerSmartisan/CleanerSmartisan.apk
stock sha256:
  8ebb1a4f6cbdea72fee4ba7bdb0ac64511183ff06aaa3cafbd83a2c6df9b504b
preflight:
  inspect=YELLOW
  replace=ORANGE
manifest:
  3 activities, 1 service, 2 exported activities, no provider
  no sysconfig reference, no privapp permissions, no overlay target
source note:
  CleanerReceiver reacts to LOCALE_CHANGED and starts CleanerService.
  The localized display fallback path in b0/m.java returns China/US names
  and falls back to a default string, so removing ja/ko resource configs keeps
  code and manifest unchanged.
graph note:
  package-specific graph queries did not surface direct overlay/sysconfig
  coupling; the general Smartisan ResourcesManager/IconManager redirection
  risk remains the standard resource-replacement gate.
```

Failed generic rebuild route:

```text
tools/r2-build-apk-locale-prune.sh \
  --package com.smartisanos.cleaner \
  --apk-only-variant v0.23a-cleaner-locale-prune-apk

result:
  failed during apktool/aapt2 rebuild

key error:
  DynamicRefTable(0x02): No mapping for build-time package ID 0x02
  res/layout/big_file_list_activity.xml:14: error: attribute smartisanos:blankStyle not found

interpretation:
  CleanerSmartisan can decode, but aapt2 cannot safely rebuild it through the
  generic path because it references Smartisan private framework attrs.
```

New binary fallback tool:

```text
tools/r2-build-apk-locale-prune-binary-arsc.sh

behavior:
  extracts stock resources.arsc
  removes non-English/non-Chinese RES_TABLE_TYPE_TYPE config chunks with
    tools/r2-arsc-prune-locales.py
  merges the pruned resources.arsc into the stock APK shell with ZIP STORED
  verifies AndroidManifest.xml and classes*.dex are byte-identical to stock
  verifies decoded resource dirs and binary locale policy
  records APK-only manifest rows when --apk-only-variant is supplied
```

Successful v0.23a build:

```text
tools/r2-build-apk-locale-prune-binary-arsc.sh \
  --package com.smartisanos.cleaner \
  --apk-only-variant v0.23a-cleaner-locale-prune-apk \
  --apk-only-note 'P1c focused source/graph-reviewed CleanerSmartisan binary resources.arsc prune after apktool rebuild failed on Smartisan private attrs; removes ja/ko only; preserves AndroidManifest.xml/classes*.dex; APK-only evidence outside ROM image and not live-tested'

output:
  hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.cleaner-locale-prune-en-zh.apk
sha256:
  d0a12dbc5bab63dbb7bba43cc01c56c91e4503fda1eaf6852b80bb50cc5639fc
stock resources.arsc:
  sha256=51b272e3959c6296c66f526d85459744e72b8fb94d70fca33e2d0d3444e6307d
pruned resources.arsc:
  sha256=4b2fa7625472069e026492b31d67a562f6bc0891abf7b65752c0e70652735a3b
removed config chunks:
  dimen:ja
  string:ja
  string:ko
kept decoded locale dirs:
  values-zh-rCN
  values-zh-rTW
signature boundary:
  keytool_status=1
  jarsigner_status=1
  SHA-256 digest error for resources.arsc
```

Batch verification:

```text
tools/r2-language-full-prune-coverage-audit.py
tools/r2-verify-apk-only-locale-prune-candidates.sh
tools/r2-locale-prune-coverage-audit.py
tools/r2-language-next-batch-plan.py
tools/r2-language-p1-source-review-audit.py
tools/r2-language-source-coupling-audit.py
tools/r2-system-mod-readiness-audit.py

APK-only verifier:
  PASS: apk-only locale-prune candidate verification
  count=11
  report=hard-rom/inspect/apk-only-locale-prune-candidates/verify-apk-only-locale-prune-candidates-20260618-144236.txt

language next-batch plan:
```

```text
No adb, no fastboot, no flash, no reboot, no erase misc, no ROM image build,
```

## 2026-06-18 - v0.25 SettingsSmartisan No-Op Gate Rebased On v0.24

Goal:

```text
Move the SettingsSmartisan core-APK no-op gate from the old v0.4 baseline onto
the current live-verified v0.24 baseline. This keeps the dark-mode route moving
without discarding the v0.24 APK-only language-prune state.
```

User priority update:

```text
Dark-mode/system behavior work now has priority over language hard-prune work.
Language remains an active goal, but it is behind native system light/dark
mode integration.
```

Tooling changes:

```text
tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.6-settings-noop.sh
  now accepts SETTINGS_NOOP_VARIANT, SETTINGS_NOOP_HELD_TAG, and BASE_SPARSE.
  Default behavior remains the historical v0.6 v0.4-based build.

tools/r2-verify-v0.6-settings-noop.sh
  now accepts SETTINGS_NOOP_VARIANT, EXPECTED_SUPER, and INSPECT_DIR so the
  same live verifier can validate rebased SettingsSmartisan no-op gates.

tools/r2-verify-settingssmartisan-offline-images.sh
  now accepts VERIFY_VARIANTS and knows v0.25-settings-noop-on-v0.24.

tools/r2-live-flash-preflight.sh
  now has a v0.25-settings-noop-on-v0.24 read-only preflight entry.

tools/r2-system-mod-readiness-audit.py
  now tracks v0.25 offline proof and the missing v0.25 live gate.

tools/r2-system-modification-route-audit.py
tools/r2-darkmode-source-coupling-audit.py
tools/r2-language-source-coupling-audit.py
  now point SettingsSmartisan core behavior gates at v0.25 instead of the old
  v0.6 route. Language-facing Settings changes are explicitly behind the
  dark-mode priority line.
```

```text
review proves a safer mechanism.
```

## 2026-06-18 launcher-entry hide audit

User target:

```text
Keep the functions but remove desktop app entries for:
  闪念胶囊
  视频播放器
  屏幕录制
  搜索
  一步
```

Read-only audit command:

```bash
tools/r2-launcher-entry-hide-audit.py
```

Generated evidence:

```text
docs/research/launcher-entry-hide-audit.md
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/launcher-entry-hide-audit.tsv
```

Static result:

```text
视频播放器:
  package=com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject
  launcher=com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject.MainActivity filter=1
  risk=ORANGE
  preserve VIEW/BROWSABLE video handlers and VideoProvider

屏幕录制:
  package=com.smartisanos.screenrecorder
  launcher=com.smartisanos.screenrecorder.EmptyActivity filter=1
  risk=ORANGE
  preserve recorder/screenshot services, settings activities, and provider

搜索:
  package=com.smartisanos.quicksearch
  launcher=com.android.quicksearchbox.SearchActivity filter=2
  risk=ORANGE
  preserve GLOBAL_SEARCH, SEARCH, launchSpeech, TNTSearchActivity, providers,
  and boot receiver

闪念胶囊:
  package=com.smartisanos.sara
  launcher=com.smartisanos.sara.bubble.SettingActivity filter=1
  risk=ORANGE
  defer behind the first manifest-only live batch because VoiceAssistant is a
  large priv-app with speech/provider/shortcut coupling

一步:
  package=com.smartisanos.sidebar
  launcher=com.smartisanos.sidebar.setting.SettingActivity filter=1
  risk=RED because sharedUserId=android.uid.system
  single-package gate only; do not batch with lower-risk packages
```

Route decision:

```text
Do not delete packages and do not use pm disable for this target.
The ROM route is manifest-only launcher-surface surgery: remove only
android.intent.category.LAUNCHER from the selected MAIN launcher filter while
keeping the activity enabled and preserving non-launcher intent filters,
services, providers, receivers, permissions, and explicit settings routes.

First proposed build candidate:
  v0.26a = VideoPlayer + ScreenRecorder + QuickSearch

Deferred:
  v0.26b = Sara / VoiceAssistant after v0.26a live proof
  later single-package RED gate = Sidebar / One Step
```

Boundary:

```text
No image was built, no APK was modified, and no flash/reboot/erase/package/data
mutation occurred. This was source/static-index planning only.
```

```text
from v0.24. Therefore the live device currently has SmartisanSystemUI no-op
installed, while SettingsSmartisan is back to the v0.24 APK rather than the

means the next behavior build must combine both real APK replacements into one
new exact-current image.

replacement boundaries, not the real toggleDarkMode behavior.

image that replaces SettingsSmartisan and SmartisanSystemUI with the already
semantically verified native dark-mode APKs, then run offline image verification
before asking for any behavior flash.
```

Objective:

```text
ROM candidate on the live-verified v0.24 line. This is the first behavior ROM
that replaces both SettingsSmartisan and SmartisanSystemUI together.
```

```text
  sha256=0d5990969cf74e5c0073e1819862688bf20a406d4d41dd8242175f4ac5575aae

manifest:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.11-native-darkmode-exact-current.SHA256SUMS.txt

offline verifier:
  hard-rom/inspect/v0.11-native-darkmode/verify-v0.11-native-darkmode-offline-image-20260618-163441.txt
```

Patched APKs:

```text
SettingsSmartisan:
  hard-rom/build/apk/SettingsSmartisan-darkmode-ui-widget.apk
  sha256=8a4472dbfe90c16dc3cdf01eb2a41bdcb951b5c0da1b07d57dba19373812a7f0
  inserted path=/system/priv-app/SettingsSmartisan/SettingsSmartisan.apk
  replacement path=shared_blocks-safe held-stock inode
  held stock path=/system/priv-app/SettingsSmartisan/.SettingsSmartisan.apk.smartisax-v011-stock-held

SmartisanSystemUI behavior APK:
  hard-rom/build/apk/SmartisanSystemUI-darkmode-tile.apk
  sha256=c80904f85acf15ca706d4a40b1dad9f5c556ff69affa7fe270a9221889a7de26

SmartisanSystemUI same-size inserted APK:
  hard-rom/build/apk/SmartisanSystemUI-darkmode-tile-samesize.apk
  sha256=42996f1c39b5a7bf3775c7da59982b385ced43a74dcb431b1973e64ffd19fe1f
  inserted path=/priv-app/SmartisanSystemUI/SmartisanSystemUI.apk
  replacement path=owner-audited debugfs rm/write same path
```

SystemUI replacement discovery:

```text
The original in-place writer was rejected for SmartisanSystemUI because the APK
inode has repeated physical block aliases. The same-size v0.11 APK does not keep
all alias groups byte-identical, so raw full-file in-place overwrite would risk
corrupt readback.

The safe offline path was then proven on a clone:
  - audit every unique physical block of the stock SystemUI APK
  - confirm all unique blocks are owned only by the SystemUI inode
  - debugfs rm the public path and write the same path back with the same-size APK
  - restore mode/uid/gid/SELinux xattr
  - e2fsck -fy followed by e2fsck -fn
  - dump the APK from the final image and verify sha256 plus unzip -t

Evidence:
  hard-rom/work/v0.11-native-darkmode/systemui-block-owner-audit.json
```

Offline verifier result:

```text
PASS: v0.11 native dark-mode offline image verification

apk_semantic_verifier=PASS
systemui_samesize_comment_len=6918
systemui_samesize_member_equivalence=ok
zip_integrity=ok

SettingsSmartisan signature boundary:
  keytool/jarsigner report SHA-256 digest error for classes.dex

SmartisanSystemUI same-size signature boundary:
  keytool/jarsigner report SHA-256 digest error for classes10.dex

dumped SettingsSmartisan APK hash:
  8a4472dbfe90c16dc3cdf01eb2a41bdcb951b5c0da1b07d57dba19373812a7f0

dumped SmartisanSystemUI APK hash:
  42996f1c39b5a7bf3775c7da59982b385ced43a74dcb431b1973e64ffd19fe1f

sparse logical slices:
  system_b image=fd78a14ba0dfde33d6c87021d7cd8aa3adebe892daba0c438c78b663670e3df9
  system_b sparse_slice=fd78a14ba0dfde33d6c87021d7cd8aa3adebe892daba0c438c78b663670e3df9
  system_ext_b image=0d5990969cf74e5c0073e1819862688bf20a406d4d41dd8242175f4ac5575aae
  system_ext_b sparse_slice=0d5990969cf74e5c0073e1819862688bf20a406d4d41dd8242175f4ac5575aae
```

Preflight result:

```text

result=PASS

  candidate sparse sha256
  v0.4 rollback sparse sha256

```

Boundary:

```text
No flash, no reboot, no fastboot erase misc, and no /data mutation happened in
this v0.11 build/verification step.

v0.11-native-darkmode is now a built and offline-verified behavior ROM
candidate. It is not live proof. The next step requires exact user confirmation

post-flash evidence section.
```

## 2026-06-18 - v0.11 Settings Row Reachability Bug Found; v0.11.1 Offline Candidate Built

Context:

```text
The reversible `/data` write test proved v0.11 at the UiModeManager and
SystemUI tile-creation level, but it did not prove that the SettingsSmartisan
Brightness page row was user-visible on R2.
```

Bug found:

```text
The v0.11 SettingsSmartisan patch inserted the hidden switch_dc row retitle/
visibility block before the :cond_5 label in
BrightnessSettingsFragment.onActivityCreated().

On Darwin/R2, stock code jumps to :cond_5 when SettingsFeature.isDarwin() is
true, so the v0.11 dark-mode row exposure can be skipped. Therefore v0.11 must
not be treated as final Settings UX proof, even though it remains valid live
proof for boot/package/hash, UiMode yes/no, and SystemUI toggleDarkMode tile
creation.
```

Fix:

```text
tools/r2-build-native-darkmode-tile-apks.sh
tools/r2-build-settingssmartisan-darkmode-ui-apk.sh
tools/r2-verify-v0.11-native-darkmode-tile-apks.sh
```

The builders now insert the row exposure after the Darwin `:cond_5` branch
target. The APK verifier now checks this explicitly with:

```text
brightness_darkmode_row_reachability=ok
```

APK verifier:

```bash
tools/r2-verify-v0.11-native-darkmode-tile-apks.sh
```

Report:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/v0.11-native-darkmode-tile/verify-v0.11-native-darkmode-tile-apks-20260618-171710.txt
hard-rom/inspect/v0.11-native-darkmode-tile/smali-evidence-20260618-171710/Settings-BrightnessSettingsFragment.smali
```

Key evidence:

```text
Settings-BrightnessSettingsFragment.smali:
  if-nez p1, :cond_5
  ...
  :cond_5
  iget-object p1, p0, ...->mReduceStrobeSwitch
  const v0, 0x7f120d5a
  invoke-virtual {p1, v0}, ...->setTitle(I)V

result:
  PASS
  brightness_darkmode_row_reachability=ok
```

```text
device. The row is visible in 屏幕和字体 and owns a real SwitchEx.

No row tap/toggle was performed in this step because that would write
UiMode/settings state under /data and requires explicit user approval.

Remaining dark-mode UX work:
  1. With explicit /data-write approval, test the Settings row toggle path and
     restore UiMode to the original no/dark-off state.
  2. Polish the row/widget title: the current SettingsSmartisan resource text
     resolves to "Dark"; a native Chinese label such as "深色模式" or
     "夜间模式" should be used before calling the UX final.
  3. Validate the Smartisan QS editor candidate behavior and then decide
     editor-first vs default-visible policy.
```

## 2026-06-18 - v0.26a Launcher Entry Hide Offline Candidate Built

User goal:

```text
Keep 闪念胶囊, 视频播放器, 屏幕录制, 搜索, and 一步 functional, but remove
their desktop app entries.
```

Staging decision:

```text
v0.26a:
  视频播放器 / VideoPlayer / com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject
  屏幕录制 / ScreenRecorderSmartisan / com.smartisanos.screenrecorder
  搜索 / QuickSearchBoxSmartisan / com.smartisanos.quicksearch

deferred:
  v0.26b = 闪念胶囊 / VoiceAssistant / com.smartisanos.sara
  single-package RED gate = 一步 / Sidebar / com.smartisanos.sidebar
```

Reason:

```text
v0.26a is the smaller first manifest-only batch without android.uid.system.
Sara is a large VoiceAssistant priv-app and Sidebar/One Step uses
sharedUserId=android.uid.system, so they should not be batched with the first
launcher-surface test.
```

APK build:

```bash
tools/r2-build-launcher-entry-hide-apks.sh --variant v0.26a
```

Output manifest:

```text
hard-rom/build/apk/launcher-entry-hide-apk-manifest.tsv
```

Patched APKs:

```text
hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject-launcher-hidden.apk
  sha256=b67c725d7b69392288a8c33aea8977ebb66b987af8cd50a6ea805857c01abe79

hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.screenrecorder-launcher-hidden.apk
  sha256=e5e1bc96e312a7e2343e14b61e252c6e1a90955d125f36c49887a3c241dadf7f

hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.quicksearch-launcher-hidden.apk
  sha256=060b90e880898de4bd1737d3c970f0d2a858fffe96be31749a6509298777490b
```

APK-level boundary:

```text
Each patched APK changes only AndroidManifest.xml in the stock APK shell.
The target MAIN intent-filter remains present, but
android.intent.category.LAUNCHER is removed from that filter.
All non-manifest ZIP members remain byte-identical to stock; native library
local-header offsets remain stable where native libraries exist.
The expected ordinary JAR verification boundary is:
  SHA-256 digest error for AndroidManifest.xml
```

ROM build:

```bash
REBUILD_APKS=0 tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.26a-launcher-entry-hide.sh
```

```text

sha256=2f1a4d8b8579551bf04246d00099f15c5c5a42146336cd6a00d129bbcffb8fa0
```

Follow-up read-only forensics:

```text
The APK files were present on /system and matched the v0.26a hashes:

/system/priv-app/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.apk
  b67c725d7b69392288a8c33aea8977ebb66b987af8cd50a6ea805857c01abe79
/system/priv-app/ScreenRecorderSmartisan/ScreenRecorderSmartisan.apk
  e5e1bc96e312a7e2343e14b61e252c6e1a90955d125f36c49887a3c241dadf7f
/system/app/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan.apk
  060b90e880898de4bd1737d3c970f0d2a858fffe96be31749a6509298777490b
```

PackageManager logcat evidence:

```text
Failed to scan /system/priv-app/ScreenRecorderSmartisan:
  Failed collecting certificates for .../ScreenRecorderSmartisan.apk
Failed to scan /system/priv-app/VideoPlayer:
  Failed collecting certificates for .../VideoPlayer.apk
Failed to scan /system/app/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan:
  Failed collecting certificates for .../QuickSearchBoxSmartisan.apk

System package com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject no longer exists; it's data will be wiped
System package com.smartisanos.quicksearch no longer exists; it's data will be wiped
System package com.smartisanos.screenrecorder no longer exists; it's data will be wiped
```

Conclusion:

```text
v0.26a boots, and the launcher entries disappear, but it fails the real goal
because the three packages are not accepted by PackageManager.

Root cause: the first manifest merge stripped the APK v2 signing block. For a
manifest edit, the remaining JAR/v1 certificate path hits:
  SHA-256 digest error for AndroidManifest.xml
and PackageManager cannot collect certificates.

This differs from prior dex-only core APK experiments where a v2 signing block
remained available as an unsafe certificate carrier on the system-partition
skipVerify path.
```

v0.26a.1 fix:

```text
tools/r2-apk-preserve-v2-signing-block.py
```

Purpose:

```text
Copy each stock APK's APK Sig Block 42 into the manifest-edited APK. This does
not make the edited payload cryptographically valid; it preserves the original
v2 certificate carrier so PackageManager's system-partition skipVerify cert
collection can try the same route used by the successful dex-edit gates.
```

v2cert APKs:

```text
hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject-launcher-hidden-v2cert.apk
  sha256=482d05dbe82611e7dedd6eed0964e85cf6882ea22709981cec101311489d2734
  apk_sig_block_magic=present

hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.screenrecorder-launcher-hidden-v2cert.apk
  sha256=36782cff3384242e1560b3f9748ce86ef426ab9e904967c1b35011db989a4e4d
  apk_sig_block_magic=present

hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.quicksearch-launcher-hidden-v2cert.apk
  sha256=deb179992f9886dbf34ba44814a7456eb26515d9bf8bc8ab33b205519477c604
  apk_sig_block_magic=present
```

## V0.26a.1 Live Result And V0.26a.2 Package-Cache Fix

v0.26a.1 was flashed to B slot on 2026-06-18 after the exact confirmation:

```text
确认刷入 v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert B 槽
```

Flash evidence:

```text

```

```text

unlocked: yes
is-userspace: no
flash super: sparse 1/9 through 9/9 OK, total 272.262s
erase misc: OKAY
reboot: OKAY
```

Boot evidence:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/boot-wait-v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-20260618-183910.txt
attempt 4: sys.boot_completed=1 slot=_b bootanim=stopped
```

Initial read-only verifier:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/verify-v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-device-20260618-183927.txt
PASS
```

That initial PASS was a false positive for the desktop-entry goal because it
ran while the device was still in the locked/FallbackHome state. After the user
unlocked the device, the real Smartisan launcher focused:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/unlock-window-check-v0.26a.1-20260618-183948.txt
mFocusedApp=com.smartisanos.launcher/.Launcher
isKeyguardShowing=false
```

The launcher query after unlock showed the three target launcher entries still
present:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/launcher-query-after-unlock-20260618-184004.txt
com.smartisanos.quicksearch/com.android.quicksearchbox.SearchActivity
com.smartisanos.screenrecorder/.EmptyActivity
com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject/.MainActivity
```

Package acceptance itself was fixed by v0.26a.1. The live packages remained
installed from `/system`, had no `/data/app` shadow, and matched the expected
v2cert APK hashes:

```text
com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject:
  /system/priv-app/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.apk
  sha256=482d05dbe82611e7dedd6eed0964e85cf6882ea22709981cec101311489d2734

com.smartisanos.screenrecorder:
  /system/priv-app/ScreenRecorderSmartisan/ScreenRecorderSmartisan.apk
  sha256=36782cff3384242e1560b3f9748ce86ef426ab9e904967c1b35011db989a4e4d

com.smartisanos.quicksearch:
  /system/app/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan.apk
  sha256=deb179992f9886dbf34ba44814a7456eb26515d9bf8bc8ab33b205519477c604
```

The device APKs were pulled and decoded after unlock:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/device-VideoPlayer-after-unlock.apk
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/device-ScreenRecorderSmartisan-after-unlock.apk
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/device-QuickSearchBoxSmartisan-after-unlock.apk
hard-rom/inspect/v0.26a.1-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert/device-apk-manifest-after-unlock-20260618-184249/
```

Decoded APK manifest result:

```text
VideoPlayer MainActivity filter 1:
  actions=[android.intent.action.MAIN]
  categories=[android.intent.category.MONKEY]

ScreenRecorder EmptyActivity filter 1:
  actions=[android.intent.action.MAIN]
  categories=[]

QuickSearch SearchActivity filter 2:
  actions=[android.intent.action.MAIN]
  categories=[]
```

Therefore v0.26a.1 was a partial failure:

```text
PASS: boot, root, package acceptance, package hashes, no /data/app shadow
FAIL: after user unlock, PackageManager/Launcher still exposed the stale
      LAUNCHER resolver entries
```

Root cause:

```text
PackageCacher.getCacheKey(File packageFile, int flags):
  packageFile.getName() + '-' + flags

PackageCacher.isCacheUpToDate(File packageFile, File cacheFile):
  return pkg.st_mtime < cache.st_mtime
```

Source:

```text
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__framework__services.jar/sources/com/android/server/pm/parsing/PackageCacher.java
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__framework__services.jar/sources/com/android/server/pm/parsing/PackageParser2.java
```

For these cluster-style package directories, PackageManager's `packageFile` is
the directory, not the APK file. Live directory mtimes were still the stock ROM
epoch:

```text
/system/priv-app/VideoPlayer                    mtime=2009-01-01 08:00:00 +0800
/system/priv-app/ScreenRecorderSmartisan        mtime=2009-01-01 08:00:00 +0800
/system/app/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan             mtime=2009-01-01 08:00:00 +0800
```

The stale parse-cache files were newer:

```text
/data/system/package_cache/cf20dfcdce6e5c9332f87b50a725fbc9207d315b/VideoPlayer-16
/data/system/package_cache/cf20dfcdce6e5c9332f87b50a725fbc9207d315b/ScreenRecorderSmartisan-16
/data/system/package_cache/cf20dfcdce6e5c9332f87b50a725fbc9207d315b/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan-16
mtime=2026-06-17 21:02:04 +0800
```

So PackageCacher treated the old ParsedPackage cache as fresh and restored the
old launcher intent filters even though the flashed APK manifests had no
LAUNCHER category.

v0.26a.2 fix:

```text
variant:
  v0.26a.2-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump

additional ROM change over v0.26a.1:
  bump package directory ctime/atime/mtime/crtime to
  0x6a33ddc0 = 2026-06-18 20:00:00 +0800

target directories:
  /system/priv-app/VideoPlayer
  /system/priv-app/ScreenRecorderSmartisan
  /system/app/QuickSearchBoxSmartisan
```

Build command:

```bash
REBUILD_APKS=0 \
IMAGE_TAG=v026a2 \
SPARSE_VARIANT=otatrust-v0.26a.2-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump-exact-current \
PURPOSE='Manifest-only launcher entry hide v2-cert fix plus package directory mtime cache invalidation for VideoPlayer, ScreenRecorderSmartisan, and QuickSearchBoxSmartisan on top of live-verified v0.11.1' \
WORK_DIR="$PWD/hard-rom/work/v0.26a.2-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump" \
SYSTEM_IMG="$PWD/hard-rom/build/system-otatrust-v0.26a.2-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump.img" \
OUT_SPARSE="$PWD/hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.26a.2-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump-exact-current.sparse.img" \
MANIFEST="$PWD/hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.26a.2-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump-exact-current.SHA256SUMS.txt" \
VIDEO_APK="$PWD/hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.videoplayerproject-launcher-hidden-v2cert.apk" \
SCREENREC_APK="$PWD/hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.screenrecorder-launcher-hidden-v2cert.apk" \
QUICKSEARCH_APK="$PWD/hard-rom/build/apk/com.smartisanos.quicksearch-launcher-hidden-v2cert.apk" \
PACKAGE_DIR_MTIME_HEX=0x6a33ddc0 \

tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.26a-launcher-entry-hide.sh
```

## 2026-06-18 - v0.26b Sara Launcher Entry Hide Built Offline And Preflighted

Goal:

```text
Keep Sara / VoiceAssistant / 闪念胶囊 installed and functional, but remove only
its desktop launcher entry. This is a manifest-only follow-up to the live-proven
v0.26a.2 package-cache-safe launcher-entry-hide route.
```

Source and risk review:

```text
Package: com.smartisanos.sara
ROM path: /system/priv-app/VoiceAssistant/VoiceAssistant.apk
stock sha256: 082c89dc312abe0dc8a22543ea714685f15f97bf122272b024d90a581e91e24b
static level: ORANGE
reason: large priv-app with providers, services, speech/shortcut coupling, and
        exported components
launcher activity: com.smartisanos.sara.bubble.SettingActivity
launcher filter: filter 1, MAIN + LAUNCHER
source finding:
  SettingActivity is only a thin subclass of RealSettingActivity.
  The manifest has no non-launcher intent filters on SettingActivity.
  res/xml-v25/shortcuts.xml keeps an explicit targetClass pointing to
  com.smartisanos.sara.bubble.SettingActivity, so preserving the activity keeps
  the explicit shortcut route available even after the launcher category is
  removed.
  SaraProvider, ForceTouchProvider, SpeechLocationTimeProvider, voice command
  activities, shell/bubble activities, accessibility services, and receivers
  remain untouched.
```

Acceptance:

```text
Status: PASS / accepted for the five-package launcher-entry-hide baseline.
v0.26c supersedes v0.26b as the current live-proven launcher-entry-hide state.
The route is now proven for VideoPlayer, ScreenRecorderSmartisan, QuickSearch,
Sara/VoiceAssistant, and Sidebar/One Step: preserve the APK/package identity,
copy the stock APK signing block as the certificate carrier, change only the
target AndroidManifest.xml launcher category, and bump the package directory
mtime so Android 11 PackageCacher reparses the manifest.

Do not generalize v0.26c to arbitrary shared-UID/system apps. Sidebar passed
because its dedicated source audit identified the launcher-only SettingActivity
surface and the live verifier proved service/provider/window preservation.
```

## 2026-06-18 - v0.27 Smartisan Cloud Service Debloat Offline PASS / Preflight PASS

Goal:

```text
Delete vendor cloud-service content from the ROM layer while keeping the current
live-proven v0.26c launcher-entry-hide baseline.
```

Source baseline:

```text
variant:
  v0.26c-sidebar-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump

  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.26c-sidebar-launcher-entry-hide-v2cert-cachebump-exact-current.sparse.img

  fa78ad42e8e8e367a61339d7bf28e4b94dba402bdfb02a944c317a1eda76c5e1
```

Static target selection:

```text
target packages:
  com.smartisanos.cloudsync
  com.smartisanos.cloudsyncshare
  com.smartisanos.cloudagent

ROM paths removed from system_b:
  /system/priv-app/CloudServiceSmartisan
  /system/priv-app/CloudServiceShare
  /system/priv-app/CloudSyncAgent

sysconfig edit:
  /system/etc/sysconfig/hiddenapi-package-whitelist.xml
    removed package entries for the three cloud packages
```

Important boundary:

```text
com.smartisanos.cloudsync is an updated-system app on the current live device:
  /data/app/.../com.smartisanos.cloudsync.../base.apk

Therefore v0.27 ROM removal alone may not produce final live package absence.
The /data package cleanup is intentionally separated behind its own explicit
approval.
```

## 2026-06-18 - Read-only Wallet + HandShaker pre-delete audit

Goal:

```text
Research whether the next hard-debloat step can remove Smartisan Wallet and
HandShaker, with special attention to whether deleting HandShaker would break
phone-to-computer connectivity.
```

Commands:

```bash
tools/r2-root.sh status
adb -s bb12d264 shell 'getprop ro.boot.slot_suffix; getprop sys.boot_completed; getprop init.svc.bootanim; getprop sys.usb.config; getprop sys.usb.state'
adb -s bb12d264 shell 'cmd package list packages -u -f' | rg -i 'wallet|hand|shake|handin|handshaker|mtp|usb|connect|transfer|smartisanlink|share'
tools/r2-rom-mod-preflight.py com.smartisanos.wallet --action inspect
tools/r2-rom-mod-preflight.py com.smartisanos.wallet --action delete
tools/r2-rom-mod-preflight.py com.smartisanos.smartfolder.aoa --action inspect
tools/r2-rom-mod-preflight.py com.smartisanos.smartfolder.aoa --action delete
adb -s bb12d264 shell 'dumpsys usb; dumpsys package com.android.mtp'
```

Live facts:

```text
slot: _b
sys.boot_completed: 1
init.svc.bootanim: stopped
root: uid=0(root), SELinux Enforcing
USB state: mtp,diag,diag_mdm,mass_storage,adb

Wallet live package:
  /data/app/.../com.smartisanos.wallet.../base.apk
  flags: SYSTEM + UPDATED_SYSTEM_APP + PRIVILEGED
  hidden stock base: /system/priv-app/WalletSmartisan

HandShaker live package:
  /system/app/HandShaker/HandShaker.apk
  package: com.smartisanos.smartfolder.aoa

Earlier removed package:
  com.smartisanos.handinhand is distinct from HandShaker and is already absent
  from the hard-debloat line.
```

Research result:

```text
report:
  docs/research/wallet-handshaker-debloat-audit.md

HandShaker:
  Not the owner of ordinary ADB/MTP/charging. It registers a Smartisan-specific
  USB accessory activity for manufacturer=Smartisan, model=HandShaker and has
  a private ConnectionManagerService for accessory/ADB_PORT/Wi-Fi transfer.
  Basic MTP belongs to com.android.mtp under /system/priv-app/MtpService, and
  live dumpsys usb reports current_functions_applied=true with ADB + MTP.

Wallet:
  Optional but broader ORANGE package because it is a priv-app with
  updated-system /data state, wallet providers, NFC/off-host payment service,
  lockscreen/home wallet shortcut hooks, and Settings/Keyguard feature gates.
  Deletion should not affect boot, ADB, MTP, launcher, telephony, or SystemUI,
  but it intentionally removes Wallet/NFC card/payment/fingerprint-wallet
  behavior.
```

Recommended next variant:

```text
v0.28-wallet-handshaker-debloat

Remove from ROM:
  /system/priv-app/WalletSmartisan
  /system/app/HandShaker

Also remove hiddenapi whitelist rows for:
  com.smartisanos.wallet
  com.smartisanos.smartfolder.aoa

Retain and verify:
  com.android.mtp
  MediaProvider
  USB init/HAL/framework surfaces
  ADB and MTP live state after boot

Post-boot /data cleanup:
  Required for com.smartisanos.wallet because the active code path is an
  updated-system package under /data/app. Do not run cleanup without explicit
  user approval.
```

Status:

```text
READ_ONLY_AUDIT_COMPLETE. No image was built, no partition was flashed, no
reboot was triggered, and no /data cleanup was performed.
```

## 2026-06-18 - Quark Browser Magic-Mod Static Planning PASS

Purpose:

```text
Evaluate the user-installed Quark Browser as a future magic-mod target before
turning it into a built-in ROM browser or launcher candidate.
```

Scope:

```text
Mac-only static analysis. No ROM image was changed, no APK was modified or
installed, no device reboot/flash/data cleanup was performed, and the phone was
not required for this pass.
```

Input APK:

```text
path:
  apks/quark/original/com.quark.browser-10.12.0.1095-vc1095-20260618-base.apk
sha256:
  a07e2b91b391dd12f612fe3fb5e81731e4c6f5414ab54bc2e1545a5a28ebbc5b
package:
  com.quark.browser
version:
  10.12.0.1095 / vc1095
```

Decode and graph artifacts:

```text
APKTool decode:
  reverse/quark-browser-10.12.0.1095/apktool/

focused corpus:
  reverse/quark-browser-10.12.0.1095/graph-corpus/

graphify model:
  reverse/quark-browser-10.12.0.1095/graph-model/quark_startup_security_model.py

graphify report:
  reverse/quark-browser-10.12.0.1095/graph-model/graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md
  result: 67 nodes, 89 edges, 12 communities, 0 token cost

research note:
  docs/research/quark-browser-mod-audit.md
```

Key findings:

```text
Quark startup is layered:
  AndroidManifest -> QuarkApplication -> TinkerApplication ->
  BrowserApplication -> QuarkLauncher.init(...)

Manifest launcher:
  com.ucpro.MainActivity has MAIN + LAUNCHER.
  Baseline manifest has no HOME category.
  MainActivity checks Build.MODEL == "OD103" and then finishes quickly, so it
  is probably a transition shell rather than the durable browser UI.

Likely real browser UI:
  com.ucpro.BrowserActivity, with WEB / BROWSER_HOME startup markers.

Re-sign risk:
  UC WebView verifier reads current package signatures/public keys.
  Tinker ShareSecurityCheck derives an MD5 from current package signatures.
  Qigsaw, SecurityGuard, UCLibLoader, and large native libraries are present.
```

Tool state:

```text
available:
  jadx 1.5.5
  APKTool 3.0.2 jar
  Homebrew OpenJDK 26.0.1
  jarsigner/keytool

not on PATH:
  apksigner
  zipalign
```

Status:

```text
STATIC_PASS_FOR_FIRST_MOD_PLANNING.

Do not promote Quark into ROM yet. The next safe gate is a no-op or near-no-op
developer-signed APK build and install/startup test as a user app. That install
step may require uninstalling the official Quark package first because the
same-package signature will not match, so it is a /data-changing action and
requires explicit user confirmation before execution.
```

## 2026-06-19 - Quark Browser No-op v2signed APK Offline PASS

Purpose:

```text
Build a no-op rebuilt Quark APK that is signed with APK Signature Scheme v2/v3,
so the next phone-connected gate can test install/startup without first making
behavior changes.
```

Scope:

```text
Mac-only offline build and verification. No APK was installed, no package was
uninstalled, no ROM image was changed, no device reboot/flash was performed,
and no /data mutation was attempted.
```

Why v2 signing is required:

```text
Quark targetSdkVersion:
  30

R2 Android 11 source evidence:
  ApkSignatureVerifier.getMinimumSignatureSchemeVersionForTargetSdk(targetSdk)
    targetSdk >= 30 -> 2

  PackageManagerService rejects packages whose signingDetails
  signatureSchemeVersion is lower than that minimum when parseFlags skip
  verification is not active.

Conclusion:
  The temporary JAR/v1-only signed rebuild is not a valid install candidate.
  Quark needs APK Signature Scheme v2 or newer for the user-app install gate.
```

Build tools:

```text
download source:
  Google Android repository index
selected package:
  build-tools_r35.0.1_macosx.zip
zip sha1:
  f4dda6855ddf1ea1a51ee3ab6587104bd0c1d727
local tools:
  third_party/android-build-tools/build-tools_r35.0.1_macosx/android-15/apksigner
  third_party/android-build-tools/build-tools_r35.0.1_macosx/android-15/zipalign
```

Commands:

```text
manual build equivalent:
  apktool b reverse/quark-browser-10.12.0.1095/apktool \
    -o reverse/quark-browser-10.12.0.1095/build/quark-noop-unsigned.apk

  zipalign -p -f 4 quark-noop-unsigned.apk quark-noop-aligned.apk

  apksigner sign --ks quark-dev-test.keystore \
    --ks-pass pass:smartisax --key-pass pass:smartisax \
    --out quark-noop-v2signed.apk quark-noop-aligned.apk

reusable scripts added:
  tools/r2-build-quark-noop-apk.sh
  tools/r2-verify-quark-noop-apk.sh
```

Artifacts:

```text
unsigned APK:
  reverse/quark-browser-10.12.0.1095/build/quark-noop-unsigned.apk
  sha256=a5ccc4a309175eb3f4fd362a14fefdf707dee0d474122f4f0e431dbc869cdd0c

aligned APK:
```

```text
the official Quark user app first, so the live gate is a /data-changing action
and still requires explicit user confirmation.
```


Offline verification result:

```text
package=com.smartisax.intro
providers=6
old_provider_authorities=absent
targeted_manifest_surfaces=absent
targeted_assets=absent
apk_sig_block_42=present
dex_count=15

```

Boundary:

```text
This is not live-proven yet. The first large prune removes manifest/system
entry surfaces and targeted bundled assets, but keeps dex/native code in place
for startup safety. Physical class/library deletion belongs after a
user-approved live install/startup/logcat gate proves the renamed browser core
survives package-name and signing changes.
```

Live feedback after user install:

```text
The first Intro APK installed successfully and coexisted with the official
Quark package, proving the package rename and dev-signed v2/v3 install gate.
However, when the user touched remaining deleted-feature surfaces, the app
reported a HUMMER error and exited. This means the first prune left shortcut /
CMS entry configs pointing at already removed feature assets.
```

## 2026-06-19 - v0.30 Browser/WebView Modernization Offline Audit PASS

Scope:

```text
Mac-local read-only offline work only. No APK donor was downloaded or adapted,
no image was built, no install/uninstall/reboot/flash was performed, and no
/data mutation was performed.
```

Goal:

```text
Start the Smartisax BrowserChrome/WebView modernization backport by turning
the stock Smartisan browser and system WebView state into a repeatable contract
checklist before any donor APK or ROM gate is attempted.
```

Tool:

```text
tools/r2-browser-webview-modernization-audit.py
```

Generated outputs:

```text
docs/research/browser-webview-modernization-audit.md
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/browser-webview-modernization-audit.tsv
```

Verification:

```text
python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-browser-webview-modernization-audit.py
python3 tools/r2-browser-webview-modernization-audit.py
```

Key stock BrowserChrome findings:

```text
package: com.android.browser
version: 9.0.6.4 / versionCode 20211218
path: /system/system/app/BrowserChrome/BrowserChrome.apk
sha256: 0304ebb69d7c29b15f7a348b62770d55d8009f9bfbea02d45741937456ab6d7c
risk: RED
providers: 13
exported components: 35
default browser surface: BrowserActivity launcher alias plus IntentDispatcher
  handlers for http/https/googlechrome/about/javascript/file/content/mhtml and
  document MIME types
preoptimized files:
  system/app/BrowserChrome/oat/arm64/BrowserChrome.odex
  system/app/BrowserChrome/oat/arm64/BrowserChrome.vdex
known failure: v0.3/v0.3.1 same-package BrowserChrome replacement reached a
  no-lockscreen/no-desktop state
```

BrowserChrome requirement:

```text
Do not build a behavior candidate yet. A future BrowserChrome candidate must
preserve provider authorities, default intent filters, SmartisanApplication,
zygotePreloadName, native/dex/assets shape, package directory mtime,
PackageCacher freshness, /data/system/icon redirection state, and oat/vdex
handling. First safe step is a BrowserChrome no-op/minimal gate after live
state capture is specified.
```

Key stock WebView findings:

```text
package: com.android.webview
version: 75.0.3770.156 / versionCode 377015630
path: /product/app/webview/webview.apk
sha256: 11e69a224da36b552f3d52d4b86ed0821c67945112df3b0579fcd0b39e0bed97
framework provider whitelist:
  config_webview_packages.xml lists only com.android.webview
WebViewLibrary:
  libwebviewchromium.so
sandboxed services:
  NUM_SANDBOXED_SERVICES=40
native ABI:
  arm64-v8a and armeabi-v7a libwebviewchromium.so
```

WebViewUpdater contract:

```text
Candidate provider must pass:
  UserPackage.hasCorrectTargetSdkVersion
  minimum version-code cohort from available-by-default providers
  providerHasValidSignature or system-app status
  WebViewFactory.getWebViewLibrary metadata lookup

Therefore a downloaded com.google.android.webview donor will not become a valid
provider just by placing it in ROM. Either framework-res
config_webview_packages.xml must list it, or the donor must be adapted to the
com.android.webview package/provider contract.
```

Smartisan-specific warning:

```text
SettingsSmartisan WebView selector delegates to IWebViewUpdateService and warns
that non-built-in WebView may break Big Bang and other WebView-based system
features. Big Bang/WebView surfaces are now explicit regression targets for the
backport.
```

Next safe gates:

```text
1. Create a read-only live-state capture plan for stock webviewupdate, package
   paths, package directory mtimes, package cache, /data/system/icon, default
   browser resolver, keyguard/launcher, and a simple WebView-using Settings
   page.
2. Implement a WebView donor APK/APKM analyzer that compares downloaded donor
   material against the v0.30 contract before any ROM image is built. Done in
   v0.30.1 below.
3. Prepare v0.31 as a stock WebView no-op or near-no-op provider gate.
4. Prepare BrowserChrome no-op only after provider authorities, default intent
   filters, oat/vdex handling, package cache, and icon redirection capture are
   specified.
```

## 2026-06-19 - v0.30.1 WebView Donor Analyzer Offline Gate PASS

Scope:

```text
Mac-local read-only offline work only. No donor was downloaded, no APK was
modified, no image was built, no install/uninstall/reboot/flash was performed,
and no /data mutation was performed.
```

Tool:

```text
tools/r2-webview-donor-audit.py
```

Purpose:

```text
Create a repeatable first-pass static gate for future modern WebView donor
material before any ROM image is built. The tool accepts a single APK, an
APKM/APKS/XAPK/ZIP archive containing split APKs, or a directory of APKs.
```

Checked gates:

```text
framework provider whitelist from config_webview_packages.xml
package identity / framework config patch boundary
Android 11 device minSdk compatibility
WebViewUpdater targetSdkVersion >= 30 gate
WebViewUpdater versionCode / 100000 cohort gate
com.android.webview.WebViewLibrary metadata
native library presence across base/split APKs
arm64-v8a runtime library presence
armeabi-v7a compatibility warning
NUM_SANDBOXED_SERVICES and SandboxedProcessService declaration count
WebView application class surface
split installation plan warning
local aapt parser completeness
```

## 2026-06-19 - v0.30.2 Browser/WebView Live-State Capture Script Prepared

Scope:

```text
Mac-local script preparation only. The script was not run against a live device
in this step. No install/uninstall/reboot/flash was performed, no settings were
written, no package cache was cleared, and no /data mutation was performed.
```

Tool:

```text
tools/r2-browser-webview-live-state-audit.sh
```

Purpose:

```text
Prepare the read-only live-state capture gate required before v0.31 WebView
stock no-op or near-no-op work. The script records stock BrowserChrome/WebView
runtime state so candidate builders can preserve package-cache, icon
redirection, resolver, WebViewUpdateService, and keyguard/launcher invariants.
```

Captured surfaces when run on device:

```text
adb/device props/root status
dumpsys/cmd webviewupdate
global/secure/system WebView settings
com.android.webview and com.android.browser package paths/state
Google WebView/Trichrome/Chrome package shadows if present
/product/app/webview and /system/app/BrowserChrome file mtimes
BrowserChrome oat/vdex file mtimes
default https resolver and browser launcher resolver
/data/system/package_cache matching browser/webview via root helper
/data/system/icon and redirection_policy.xml browser/webview evidence
current keyguard/launcher window state
recent Browser/WebView/PackageCacher/Smartisan resource logs
```

Local verification:

```text
bash -n tools/r2-browser-webview-live-state-audit.sh
tools/r2-browser-webview-live-state-audit.sh --help
```

Boundary:

```text
The script is read-only, but it uses the project root helper for privileged
reads under /data/system when available. v0.31 has now been built offline as a
stock near-noop image, so running this capture on the live device should
precede v0.31 flashing/live verification and any donor-backed WebView work. A
successful live report is not itself flash authorization; it is input evidence
for the next no-op gate.
```

## 2026-06-19 - v0.30.3 WebView Donor Adaptation Audit Enhanced PASS

Scope:

```text
Mac-local offline tooling/report work only. No donor was downloaded, no APK was
modified, no image was built, no install/uninstall/reboot/flash was performed,
no settings were written, and no /data mutation was performed.
```

Tool:

```text
tools/r2-webview-donor-audit.py
```

Purpose:

```text
Upgrade the WebView donor gate from a basic provider-validity checker into a
route-aware adaptation audit for Smartisax/R2. The auditor now distinguishes
simple adapt-in-place WebView replacement from framework provider config
patches and Trichrome/static shared-library multi-package designs.
```

New checked gates:

```text
bundle_package_shape:
  one package plus optional splits is the simple product/app WebView route;
  multiple packages require a dedicated ROM layout.

android11_factory_provider_class:
  R2 framework.jar WebViewFactory loads
  com.android.webview.chromium.WebViewChromiumFactoryProviderForR.
  The donor must provide that class in dex.

static_library_dependencies:
  uses-static-library / TrichromeLibrary donors are not single-APK replacements;
  they require bundling and validating the provider plus library packages,
  versions, and cert digests.

recommended_adaptation_route:
  emits adapt-in-place, framework-provider-add, or blocked/config-needed route
  plus required ROM surfaces: package directory mtime, stale oat/vdex handling,
  relro/webviewupdate verification, Settings selector, and Smartisan Big
  Bang/WebView regression checks.
```

Source evidence used:

```text
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__framework__services.jar/sources/com/android/server/webkit/WebViewUpdater.java
  validityResult():
    UserPackage.hasCorrectTargetSdkVersion
    versionCodeGE(packageInfo.getLongVersionCode(), getMinimumVersionCode())
    providerHasValidSignature(...)
    WebViewFactory.getWebViewLibrary(...)

reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__framework__framework.jar/sources/android/webkit/WebViewFactory.java
  CHROMIUM_WEBVIEW_FACTORY =
    com.android.webview.chromium.WebViewChromiumFactoryProviderForR

reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__priv-app__SettingsSmartisan__SettingsSmartisan.apk/sources/com/android/settings/WebViewImplementationFragment.java
  Settings UI reads IWebViewUpdateService.getValidWebViewPackages()
  and changeProviderAndSetting(...).
```

Verification:

```text
python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-webview-donor-audit.py

tools/r2-webview-donor-audit.py --label stock-webview-selftest
  verdict=PASS

tools/r2-webview-donor-audit.py \
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/raw/system/system/app/BrowserChrome/BrowserChrome.apk \
  --label stock-browser-as-webview-negative
  verdict=FAIL

tools/r2-webview-donor-audit.py \
  hard-rom/work/v0.31-webview-stock-near-noop/verify/offline-product-webview.apk \
  --label v0.31-webview-stock-near-noop-dumped-webview
  verdict=PASS
```

Updated reports:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-donor/stock-webview-selftest/webview-donor-audit.md
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-donor/stock-webview-selftest/webview-donor-audit.tsv
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-donor/stock-webview-selftest/webview-donor-audit.json
```

Boundary:

```text
This still does not provide a modern donor APK and does not authorize a donor
ROM image. The next donor-backed step is to run this enhanced auditor on the
actual modern WebView APK/APKM/APKS/XAPK bundle. A framework-provider-add route
or Trichrome/static-library route must be treated as a separate multi-gate ROM
design, not as a direct stock APK overwrite.
```

## 2026-06-19 - v0.30.5 WebView Donor Source Plan Prepared

Scope:

```text
Mac-local offline tooling/report work only. Public metadata was checked in the
browser for the official Google Play WebView listings and Chrome for Developers
WebView overview. No donor APK was downloaded, no image was built, no
install/uninstall/reboot/flash was performed, no settings were written, and no
/data mutation was performed.
```

Tool:

```text
tools/r2-webview-donor-source-plan.py
```

Generated outputs:

```text
docs/research/webview-donor-source-plan.md
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/webview-donor-source-plan.tsv
```

Source snapshot:

```text
Google Play stable com.google.android.webview:
  Android System WebView is a Google LLC system component and the public
  listing was updated on 2026-06-17 in the 2026-06-19 snapshot.

Google Play dev com.google.android.webview.dev:
  Dev channel says it updates weekly and the public listing was updated on
  2026-06-18 in the 2026-06-19 snapshot.

Chrome for Developers WebView overview:
  WebView is Chromium-based, shares Chrome for Android's rendering engine, and
  has an APK that can be updated separately from Android.
```

Route plan:

```text
Route A / P0:
  stable or source-built com.android.webview donor, adapt-in-place under
  /product/app/webview after v0.31 live proof.

Route B / P1:
  com.google.android.webview stable donor, framework-provider-add route.

Route C / P2:
  Trichrome/static shared-library bundle, multi-package ROM design.

Route D / P3:
  self-built Chromium/AOSP WebView targeting com.android.webview.

Route E:
  reject Chrome/Browser APKs as WebView donors; BrowserChrome remains a
  separate later no-op gate.
```

Version and compatibility rules:

```text
rules=12, including:
  stable channel first
  donor versionCode / 100000 >= stock factory cohort
  targetSdkVersion >= 30 and minSdkVersion <= 30
  WebViewLibrary metadata plus matching libwebviewchromium.so
  Android 11 WebViewChromiumFactoryProviderForR class
  arm64-v8a required, armeabi-v7a preferred until live evidence says otherwise
  com.google.android.webview needs framework-provider-add or package adaptation
  uses-static-library/Trichrome means a multi-package ROM design
  v0.31 stock provider live proof remains before donor-backed images
```

Verification:

```text
python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-webview-donor-source-plan.py
chmod +x tools/r2-webview-donor-source-plan.py
tools/r2-webview-donor-source-plan.py
  routes=5
  rules=12
```

Boundary:

```text
This report still does not provide a donor APK and does not authorize a ROM
image. It narrows what counts as a valid next donor: first prefer a stable
com.android.webview or source-built equivalent; treat com.google.android.webview
and Trichrome/static-library packages as framework/multi-package routes.
```

## 2026-06-19 - v0.30.6 Trichrome Bundle Audit Prepared

Scope:

```text
Mac-local offline tooling/report work only. No donor APK was downloaded, no
image was built, no install/uninstall/reboot/flash was performed, no settings
were written, and no /data mutation was performed.
```

Tools:

```text
tools/r2-webview-trichrome-bundle-audit.py
tools/r2-webview-donor-inbox-audit.py
tools/r2-webview-donor-source-plan.py
```

Purpose:

```text
Add the dedicated package-group gate needed for modern Google WebView donor
shapes that use Trichrome/static shared libraries. The existing donor audit
remains the single-provider compatibility gate; the new bundle audit answers
whether the input is a standalone WebView APK, a multi-package Trichrome
bundle, or not a WebView bundle at all.
```

Checks added:

```text
exactly one WebView provider candidate
standalone vs Trichrome/static-library classification
one base APK per package plus optional splits
framework provider route: com.android.webview vs framework-provider-add
provider minSdk/targetSdk compatibility
WebViewLibrary metadata and matching native library
Android 11 WebViewChromiumFactoryProviderForR dex class
arm64-v8a WebView native library presence
uses-static-library resolution to bundled library packages
TrichromeLibrary package presence
static-library version match when declared
certDigest match when local signer tooling is available
local aapt parser completeness
```

Generated outputs:

```text
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-trichrome-bundle/stock-webview-standalone/trichrome-bundle-audit.md
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-trichrome-bundle/stock-webview-standalone/trichrome-bundle-audit.tsv

hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-trichrome-bundle/stock-browser-negative/trichrome-bundle-audit.tsv
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-trichrome-bundle/stock-browser-negative/trichrome-bundle-audit.json

hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-donor-inbox/webview-donor-inbox-audit.md
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-donor-inbox/webview-donor-inbox-audit.tsv
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-donor-inbox/webview-donor-inbox-audit.json

docs/research/webview-donor-source-plan.md
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/webview-donor-source-plan.tsv
```

Verification:

```text
python3 -m py_compile \
  tools/r2-webview-trichrome-bundle-audit.py \
  tools/r2-webview-donor-inbox-audit.py \
  tools/r2-webview-donor-source-plan.py

  verdict=PASS_STANDALONE
  classification=standalone-webview

tools/r2-webview-trichrome-bundle-audit.py \
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/raw/system/system/app/BrowserChrome/BrowserChrome.apk \
  --label stock-browser-negative
  verdict=FAIL
  classification=not-webview-bundle

tools/r2-webview-donor-inbox-audit.py \
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/raw/product/app/webview/webview.apk \
  --all-apks
  candidate_count=1
  PASS com.android.webview
  bundle gate: PASS_STANDALONE / standalone-webview

tools/r2-webview-donor-inbox-audit.py --include-downloads
  candidate_count=0

tools/r2-webview-donor-source-plan.py
  routes=5
  rules=13
```

Current result:

```text
Stock WebView is now proven by both gates: the single-provider donor audit
passes, and the package-group audit classifies it as standalone rather than
Trichrome. BrowserChrome is rejected by both WebView donor logic and the new
bundle gate.

The inbox scanner now delegates every future APK/APKM/APKS/XAPK/ZIP candidate
to both the donor audit and the Trichrome bundle audit. The current local
project/Downloads scan still finds no external modern donor package.
```

Known local tooling note:

```text
apksigner exists in the local Android build-tools bundle, but the current Mac
environment reports no Java Runtime. For standalone/no-static-library stock
WebView, certificate digest extraction is not required. For a future
Trichrome/static-library donor with certDigest refs, missing signer digest
evidence is a warning that must be resolved or explicitly accepted before image
design.
```

Boundary:

```text
This report still does not provide a modern donor APK and does not authorize a
ROM image. Route C is now concrete enough to audit when a donor bundle exists,
but v0.31 stock provider live-state capture and live verification still precede
any donor-backed WebView integration candidate.
```

```text

offline WebView planning work, but do not flash v0.31 or build any donor-backed
WebView image until Browser/WebView live-state capture succeeds, v0.31 is
explicitly confirmed/flashed/live-verified, and actual modern donor material
passes donor plus Trichrome/static-library audits.
```

## 2026-06-19 - v0.32 BrowserChrome Stock Near-Noop Offline Image PASS

Scope:

```text
Mac-local offline build and verifier only.
```

Purpose:

```text
Prepare the first BrowserChrome no-op/near-noop ROM gate before any browser
behavior or engine replacement. The image keeps the stock BrowserChrome APK
byte-identical and changes only the package directory timestamp for
PackageCacher/default-browser freshness validation.
```

```text

package path: /system/app/BrowserChrome/BrowserChrome.apk
stock BrowserChrome APK sha256:
  0304ebb69d7c29b15f7a348b62770d55d8009f9bfbea02d45741937456ab6d7c

  default browser resolver state, exported providers/components, native
```

```text
hash/path, and BrowserChrome package-cache behavior.
```


Generated evidence:

```text
docs/research/webview-framework-contract-audit.md
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/webview-framework-contract-audit.tsv
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-framework-contract/webview-framework-contract-audit.json

Updated downstream plans:
docs/research/webview-integration-plan.md
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/webview-integration-plan.tsv
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-integration-plan/webview-integration-plan.json
docs/research/webview-rom-design-plan.md
reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/webview-rom-design-plan.tsv
hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-rom-design-plan/webview-rom-design-plan.json
```

Key contract:

```text
framework whitelist:
  config_webview_packages.xml has one provider:
  com.android.webview, description=Android WebView, availableByDefault=true,
  fallback=false, signatures=0.

SystemImpl boot invariants:
  at least one available-by-default provider is required.
  at most one fallback provider is allowed.

WebViewUpdater validity gates:
  targetSdkVersion >= 30.
  longVersionCode is compared by versionCode / 100000 cohort.
  stock floor cohort is 377015630 / 100000 = 3770.
  ROM system apps pass the signature gate even with no configured signatures.
  WebViewFactory.getWebViewLibrary(applicationInfo) must be non-null.

WebViewFactory / runtime gates:
  framework loads com.android.webview.chromium.WebViewChromiumFactoryProviderForR.
  provider manifest must expose com.android.webview.WebViewLibrary.
  stock WebView uses libwebviewchromium.so.
  stock native libs include arm64-v8a and armeabi-v7a libwebviewchromium.so.
  stock sandbox service count matches metadata: 40/40.
  stock privileged service count matches metadata: 0/0.

SettingsSmartisan:
  WebView selector delegates to IWebViewUpdateService.getValidWebViewPackages()
  and filters by Utils.isPackageEnabled().
  Settings resources warn that non-built-in WebView can break Smartisan
  Big Bang/WebView-based system surfaces.
```

Decision:

```text
framework_contract_audit=PASS

Route A remains the preferred first donor-backed modernization route:
adapt or source-build a standalone com.android.webview-compatible provider in
place under /product/app/webview after v0.31 live proof.

Direct com.google.android.webview is still Route B, not a drop-in path, because
it needs framework provider XML work or package adaptation. Trichrome/static
library donors remain Route C multi-package designs, not single-APK
replacements.
```

Downstream plan state:

```text
tools/r2-webview-integration-plan.py:
  framework_contract_audit=PASS
  v0.31_offline_provider_gate=PASS
  browser_webview_live_state_capture=MISSING
  v0.31_live_provider_gate=MISSING
  modern_donor_inbox=MISSING
  candidates=1
  build_ready=0

tools/r2-webview-rom-design-plan.py:
  framework_contract_audit=PASS
  design=stock-webview-baseline
  status=DESIGN_ONLY
  ready_for_design_review=0
```

Boundary:

```text
This audit is not donor material and not image build authorization. It converts
the local R2 framework into donor acceptance gates. Donor-backed image work
still requires real donor material, donor/bundle PASS, v0.31 live proof, and
explicit user confirmation before any flash.
```

## 2026-06-19 - ADB Recovery and Browser/WebView Live Baseline PASS

Context:

```text
After the device was reconnected and USB debugging was confirmed enabled, adb
was initially invisible or unstable from the Mac. Restarting the Mac-side adb
server outside the stale state restored the device. The issue did not match the
previous HandShaker debloat as the primary cause, because the live USB function
is now mtp,adb and adbd is running.
```

```text
adb devices -l
adb -s bb12d264 shell 'getprop sys.boot_completed; getprop ro.boot.slot_suffix; getprop sys.usb.config; getprop sys.usb.state; getprop init.svc.adbd; getenforce'
```

```text
webview_dirty=false

browser_path=/system/app/BrowserChrome/BrowserChrome.apk
browser_package=com.android.browser 9.0.6.4 / 20211218
https_resolver=android/smartisanos.app.ResolverActivity
keyguard=isKeyguardShowing=false
live_audit_result=PASS_READ_ONLY
```

Boundary:

```text
This is a read-only baseline capture only. It does not flash, reboot, clear
caches, write settings, mutate packages, or clean /data. It proves the current
v0.30-era B-slot runtime is adb-visible, rooted, boot-complete, launcher-visible,
and using the stock M75 com.android.webview provider. It also proves that future
Route A WebView work must preserve the com.android.webview provider contract or
explicitly modify the framework provider allowlist.
```

```text
itself. The next concrete offline step is to obtain a real source-built
SystemWebView.apk from an isolated Linux builder, copy it plus build metadata
into apks/webview-donor-inbox/, then run the Route A candidate audit. Any
future command that touches the live R2 over adb/fastboot must run outside the
sandbox with explicit confirmation for device-changing steps.
```

## 2026-06-19 - WebView Source-Built Dist Provenance Gate READY

Purpose:

```text
Tighten the Mac-side intake boundary before any returned Linux-builder
SystemWebView.apk can enter signing-transition and Route A candidate audits.
This stayed offline: it did not run Chromium fetch/build, download donors,
build ROM images, touch the device, flash, reboot, erase misc, write settings,
or modify /data.
```

Tool updates:

```text
tools/r2-webview-sourcebuilt-intake.py
  adds INTAKE-00 source-built dist provenance validation
  adds --validate-only
  validates artifact-manifest.json, SHA256SUMS.txt, args.gn,
  chromium-revision.txt, and gn-args-expanded.txt for dist inputs
  warns but does not fully trust a lone APK input because provenance metadata is
  missing

tools/r2-webview-linux-builder-kit.py
  collection script now writes richer artifact-manifest.json fields:
  gn_target, out_dir, chromium_revision, and sha256s
```

## 2026-06-19 - WebView GitHub/Self-Hosted Builder Workflow READY

Purpose:

```text
Make the missing Linux source-build step runnable on a large external builder
without relying on ignored local hard-rom/inspect artifacts being present in
Git. This stayed off-device: it did not run Chromium fetch/build locally,
download donors, build ROM images, touch the phone, flash, reboot, erase misc,
write settings, or modify /data.
```

Tool and workflow updates:

```text
tools/r2-webview-linux-builder-kit.py
  embeds the current fallback release pin:
    Chromium Android Stable 150.0.7871.28
    commit 48db307645dcbaa0bb5ccee0cd096cf22971bb84
  keeps generated kit output under ignored hard-rom/inspect
  emits preflight-linux-builder.sh
  preflight checks Linux/x86-64, required base commands, no-space build root,
  free disk, RAM, and build-root writability
  if /mnt-style build root creation fails, preflight tries a narrow sudo
  mkdir/chown fallback and then verifies the directory is writable

.github/workflows/webview-source-build.yml
  manual workflow_dispatch only
  first runs python3 tools/r2-webview-linux-builder-kit.py on the runner
  validates generated scripts with bash -n
  defaults to mode=preflight
  full mode=build uploads only the returned dist/logs
  expected runner is large self-hosted Linux x86-64 or GitHub larger Ubuntu,
  not standard ubuntu-latest

docs/research/webview-github-builder-workflow.md
  records the workflow boundary, runner requirements, preflight behavior,
  generated dist paths, and Mac-side local intake command
```

Builder script fixes made before the live run:

```text
tools/r2-webview-linux-builder-kit.py now emits:
  INSTALL_BUILD_DEPS_ARGS="${INSTALL_BUILD_DEPS_ARGS:---android --no-prompt}"
  build/install-build-deps.sh $INSTALL_BUILD_DEPS_ARGS
  git fetch --depth=1 origin "$CHECKOUT_REVISION"
  git checkout -B "smartisax-webview-${CHROMIUM_VERSION}" FETCH_HEAD
  git -C "$DEPOT_TOOLS_DIR" fetch origin main
  git -C "$DEPOT_TOOLS_DIR" checkout -B main origin/main

Rationale:
  keep Chromium dependency installation Android-scoped/noninteractive
  avoid deep tag fetch stalls
  avoid depot_tools detached-HEAD pull failures
```

Follow-up failure and retry:

```text
15:14 Asia/Shanghai:
  The first live ECS build exited before producing SystemWebView.apk.
  Failure phase: gclient sync --no-history
  Failing dependency: src/third_party/crabbyavif/src
  Remote: chromium.googlesource.com/external/github.com/webmproject/CrabbyAvif.git
  Error: HTTP 429 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
  Quota detail: Short term server-time rate limit exceeded for ECS public IP.

Response:
  tools/r2-webview-linux-builder-kit.py now emits:
    GCLIENT_SYNC_ARGS="${GCLIENT_SYNC_ARGS:---no-history --jobs 4}"
    gclient sync $GCLIENT_SYNC_ARGS

  Rationale:
    lower gclient fetch fan-out for fresh cloud IPs and retry against the same
    partially populated /mnt/webview-build workspace instead of discarding the
    downloaded source cache.

  Remote kit was re-uploaded to the ECS builder.
  A new tmux retry was started with a 300-second cooldown and:
    GCLIENT_SYNC_ARGS="--no-history --jobs 4"
```

Profile-gated build failures and fixes:

```text
The --jobs 4 retry passed gclient sync and gclient runhooks, then reached
GN/Ninja. It failed first because V8 builtins PGO profiles were missing:

  missing: v8/tools/builtins-pgo/profiles/x64.profile

Evidence:
  .gclient custom_vars was empty.
  DEPS default has checkout_pgo_profiles=False.
  DEPS only downloads V8 builtins profiles when checkout_pgo_profiles is true.

Fix applied:
  python3 v8/tools/builtins-pgo/download_profiles.py \
    download \
    --depot-tools third_party/depot_tools \
    --check-v8-revision \
    --quiet

Result:
  x64.profile, x64-rl.profile, x86.profile, x86-rl.profile, and meta.json
  were downloaded under v8/tools/builtins-pgo/profiles/.

The next Ninja resume failed because Android AFDO was missing:

  missing: chrome/android/profiles/afdo.prof

Fix applied:
  python3 tools/download_optimization_profile.py \
    --newest_state=chrome/android/profiles/newest.txt \
    --local_state=chrome/android/profiles/local.txt \
    --output_name=chrome/android/profiles/afdo.prof \
    --gs_url_base=chromeos-prebuilt/afdo-job/llvm

Result:
  chrome/android/profiles/afdo.prof was downloaded.

The Android arm32 PGO profile was also missing when queried:

  missing: chrome/build/pgo_profiles/chrome-android32-*.profdata

Fix applied:
  python3 tools/update_pgo_profiles.py \
    --target=android-arm32 \
    update \
    --gs-url-base=chromium-optimization-profiles/pgo_profiles

Already present:
  chrome/android/orderfiles/arm64/pgo_profile.arm64.profdata

Also downloaded:
  python3 tools/update_pgo_profiles.py \
    --target=android-desktop-x64 \
    update \
    --gs-url-base=chromium-optimization-profiles/pgo_profiles
```

Builder kit update:

```text
tools/r2-webview-linux-builder-kit.py now explicitly fetches:
  Android AFDO profile
  Android Arm AFDO profile
  Android arm32 PGO profile
  Android desktop x64 PGO profile
  V8 builtins PGO profiles
```

## 2026-06-19 - WebView application_class WARN resolved offline

Goal:

```text
Review whether the source-built M150 WebView Application class
org.chromium.android_webview.nonembedded.WebViewApkApplication is a real R2
Android 11 incompatibility or only a modern Chromium standalone WebView shape.
Keep the ECS builder running for possible rebuilds.
```

Evidence reviewed:

```text
Stock M75 WebView:
  application_class=com.android.webview.chromium.WebViewApplication
  source=reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/product__app__webview__webview.apk/sources/com/android/webview/chromium/WebViewApplication.java
  finding=only a narrow Android 8.x font workaround; not active on R2 Android 11

R2 framework:
  WebViewFactory loads:
    com.android.webview.chromium.WebViewChromiumFactoryProviderForR
  WebViewFactory/WebViewLibraryLoader/WebViewZygoteInit use package identity,
  WebViewLibrary metadata, provider classloader, native library, and provider
  factory class; they do not hardcode the stock Application class.

ECS Chromium source check:
  instance_id=i-t4n3boze247s8f5wzuwc
  read-only Cloud Assistant invocations:
    t-sgp6ofko007qygw
    t-sgp6ofkp2o95og0
  args.gn:
    system_webview_package_name = "com.android.webview"
  Chromium manifest:
    android_webview/nonembedded/java/AndroidManifest.xml defaults
    android:name to
    org.chromium.android_webview.nonembedded.WebViewApkApplication
  Chromium source:
    WebViewApkApplication is documented as the Application subclass for
    SystemWebView and Trichrome provider-owned processes.
```

Tooling update:

```text
tools/r2-webview-donor-audit.py now accepts both known compatible shapes:
  com.android.webview.chromium.WebViewApplication
  org.chromium.android_webview.nonembedded.WebViewApkApplication

The audit also verifies that the declared Application class is present in dex.
```

Generated report:

```text
docs/research/webview-application-class-audit.md
```

## 2026-06-20 - WebView A-SIG PackageManager offline acceptance recorded

Goal:

```text
Resolve the remaining A-SIG carrier proof for the Route A source-built M150
WebView candidate without touching the device. The question was whether the
stock-carrier APK is acceptable to the R2 Android 11 system-partition
PackageManager scan path, not whether it is a normal user-installable or fully
cryptographically re-signed APK.
```

Device actions:

```text
none
no flash, reboot, fastboot, adb, /data mutation, or image build
```

Scripts added/updated:

```text
tools/r2-webview-a-sig-package-manager-audit.py
tools/r2-webview-signing-transition-plan.py
tools/r2-webview-integration-plan.py
tools/r2-webview-rom-design-plan.py
tools/r2-webview-donor-target-matrix.py
tools/r2-webview-sourcebuilt-intake.py
```

Commands:

```text
python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-webview-a-sig-package-manager-audit.py
tools/r2-webview-a-sig-package-manager-audit.py

python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-webview-signing-transition-plan.py
tools/r2-webview-signing-transition-plan.py \
  --candidate apks/webview-donor-inbox/sourcebuilt-system-webview-150-0-7871-28/SystemWebView.apk

python3 -m py_compile \
  tools/r2-webview-integration-plan.py \
  tools/r2-webview-rom-design-plan.py \
  tools/r2-webview-donor-target-matrix.py
tools/r2-webview-integration-plan.py
tools/r2-webview-rom-design-plan.py
tools/r2-webview-donor-target-matrix.py
```

Source evidence:

```text
PackagePartitions:
  /product is in SYSTEM_PARTITIONS as PARTITION_PRODUCT.

PackageManagerService:
  system scan uses parseFlags including PARSE_IS_SYSTEM_DIR (16).
  addForInitLI() maps parseFlags & 16 to scanSystemPartition.
  scanSystemPartition causes collectCertificatesLI(..., skipVerify=true).
  the targetSdk minimum signature-scheme check is skipped when parseFlags & 16
  is set.

ParsingPackageUtils:
  skipVerify=true calls ApkSignatureVerifier.unsafeGetCertsWithoutVerification().

ApkSignatureVerifier / ApkSignatureSchemeV3Verifier:
  verifyFull=false uses the v3/v2 cert-only path and skips APK content digest
  verification. The signer block signature and cert/public-key relationship are
  still parsed/checked by the audit script.

PackageManagerServiceUtils / PackageCacher:
  parsed signingDetails are still compared against existing package state, so
  the stock-carrier must expose the stock WebView cert. /product/app/webview
  directory mtime still needs bumping to avoid stale package_cache reuse.
```

APK evidence:

```text
stock_webview:
  path=reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/raw/product/app/webview/webview.apk
  sha256=11e69a224da36b552f3d52d4b86ed0821c67945112df3b0579fcd0b39e0bed97
  apksigner=PASS_FULL_VERIFY
  Android-style unsafe preferred scheme=v3
  cert_sha256=4e95c9164652e2d13a52294d2b65603bc317bb95fd3f0b81d4d76c8dc8e5fdb1

sourcebuilt_webview:
  path=apks/webview-donor-inbox/sourcebuilt-system-webview-150-0-7871-28/SystemWebView.apk
  sha256=582e602b3ac554b4f8d1920bd2e51a61d506f933fd296b93930540cc8a6a2fd7
  apksigner=PASS_FULL_VERIFY
  Android-style unsafe preferred scheme=v2
  cert_sha256=32a2fc74d731105859e5a85df16d95f102d85b22099b8064c5d8915c61dad1e0
  interpretation=different cert, unsafe for direct same-package replacement

stock_carrier_webview:
  path=apks/webview-donor-inbox/sourcebuilt-system-webview-150-0-7871-28/SystemWebView-stock-carrier.apk
  sha256=2e2b2c3c05ba7ef40ba7fc5cc71cdde2cc09d4afd4a09ff385be04b7959d8e95
  apksigner=FAIL_FULL_VERIFY_EXIT_1
  apksigner error=APK Signature Scheme v3 CHUNKED_SHA256 digest mismatch
  Android-style unsafe preferred scheme=v3
  cert_sha256=4e95c9164652e2d13a52294d2b65603bc317bb95fd3f0b81d4d76c8dc8e5fdb1
  interpretation=expected full-verification failure, but suitable as a
  system-partition stock-cert carrier pending live proof
```

## 2026-06-20 02:02 - v0.33 system_b Partition/Footer Growth Offline PASS

Scope:

```text
offline image build and verification only
no flash
no reboot
no fastboot write
no adb write
no /data modification
```

Goal:

```text
Test the first dynamic-partition expansion gate before using system_b growth
as a WebView capacity source.
```

Important correction from the first attempt:

```text
Do not treat system_b as a plain ext4 file.

Original system_b.img contains an AVB footer and vbmeta/hashtree/FEC tail:
  image size:          3049058304 bytes
  original image size: 3000860672 bytes
  vbmeta offset:       3048407040
  vbmeta size:         896 bytes
  FEC num roots:       2

The initial truncate + resize2fs attempt cut the image down to a pure ext4
size and removed the AVB tail. That cannot be considered a safe no-content
growth gate.
```

Corrected v0.33 design:

```text
variant:
  v0.33-system-b-grow-noop
base:
  v0.31-webview-stock-near-noop live-verified sparse super
change:
  grow only system_b logical partition image by 134217728 bytes
  use avbtool resize_image to move the existing AVB footer to the new tail
  keep ext4 block count and all filesystem contents unchanged
  rebuild full super with lpmake
not changed:
  no APK bytes
  no package directories
  no ext4 filesystem growth
  no /system df growth expected
```

Commands:

```text
bash -n tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.33-system-b-grow-noop.sh \
  tools/r2-verify-v0.33-system-b-grow-noop.sh

tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.33-system-b-grow-noop.sh
tools/r2-verify-v0.33-system-b-grow-noop.sh --offline-image
```

Outputs:

```text
super sparse:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.33-system-b-grow-noop.sparse.img
  sha256=39e39965290b68a8980df8eaa090c2440000967f2f80648dc6a7316753165767

  sha256=7b778bb262e6047d8074491b1c5da54fd79c1192163dc5f6308dc616deca2c9f

manifest:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.33-system-b-grow-noop.SHA256SUMS.txt

  hard-rom/inspect/v0.33-system-b-grow-noop/verify-v0.33-system-b-grow-noop-offline-image-20260620-020849.txt
```

Boundary:

```text
v0.33 is ready for preflight and explicit-confirmation live flashing, but it is
not yet a usable /system free-space expansion. It should only prove that the R2
boots with a larger system_b dynamic partition image and moved AVB footer.

If v0.33 live PASSes, the next expansion step can be a separate filesystem
capacity gate. That later gate must either rebuild hashtree/FEC correctly or
explicitly accept a no-FEC/disabled-verity variant based on live vbmeta state.
```

## 2026-06-20 12:52 - v0.35 WebView M150 System-provider Offline Image PASS + Preflight PASS

Goal:

```text
Design and build the first donor-backed WebView modernization candidate on top
of the live-proven v0.34 FEC system_b capacity baseline. The original
product_b-only WebView replacement layout remains capacity-blocked, so this
candidate relocates the same-package provider to system_b while hiding the old
product public WebView APK from PackageManager scanning.
```

Boundary:

```text
No flash, reboot, misc erase, settings write, package mutation, package-cache
clear, or /data cleanup was performed. This is an offline/preflight candidate
only. It is not live-proven.
```

```text
variant:
  v0.35-webview-m150-system-provider
source baseline:
  v0.34-system-b-ext4-grow-fec
source sparse:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.34-system-b-ext4-grow-fec.sparse.img
```

Result:

```text
v0.35 has passed the first real modern WebView provider live gate. The phone
boots on B slot, root remains available, PackageManager accepts the relocated
system_b `com.android.webview` provider, WebViewUpdateService selects M150 with
clean relro state, BrowserChrome remains byte-identical, and launcher/keyguard
state is normal.

This is not yet a full user-facing acceptance of WebView modernization. The
remaining checks are manual/functional: open modern pages through BrowserChrome
and embedded WebView surfaces, inspect the Settings WebView implementation
selector, and exercise Smartisan Big Bang/WebView-dependent features. Keep
v0.34 and v0.4 rollback paths ready while doing those tests.
```

## 2026-06-20 13:14 - v0.35 BrowserChrome White-loading Regression Captured

After v0.35 passed the read-only WebView provider gate, user-facing functional
testing found that stock BrowserChrome could not render pages. It showed a
white page and persistent loading spinner. Smartisan Big Bang still worked.

Evidence:

```text
current-state report:
  hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-v035-regression/current-state-20260620-131346.txt
repro report:
  hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-v035-regression/browserchrome-repro-20260620-131404.txt
screenshot:
  hard-rom/inspect/browser-webview-v035-regression/browserchrome-repro-20260620-131404.png
```

Relevant facts:

```text
Settings WebView selector action:
  android.settings.WEBVIEW_SETTINGS
component:
  com.android.settings/.WebViewImplementation
direct adb open command:
  adb -s bb12d264 shell am start -a android.settings.WEBVIEW_SETTINGS

BrowserChrome focus:
  mCurrentFocus=Window{... com.android.browser/com.android.browser.BrowserActivity}

Crash:
  com.android.browser:sandboxed_process1:org.chromium.content.app.SmartisanSandboxedProcessService1:4
  Check failed: status <= ClassStatus::kLast ... at /system/app/BrowserChrome/oat/arm64/BrowserChrome.odex
  Abort message: 'Check failed: status <= ClassStatus::kLast ... at /system/app/BrowserChrome/oat/arm64/BrowserChrome.odex'
```

Interpretation:

```text
WebViewUpdateService selected M150 cleanly and Big Bang remained normal, so the
new com.android.webview provider is not globally unusable. BrowserChrome's own
sandbox renderer is crashing while loading the stock browser prebuilt odex.
The next smallest candidate is to remove BrowserChrome oat/vdex while keeping
BrowserChrome.apk and WebView M150 unchanged.
```

First build attempt note:

```text
The initial v0.35.2 builder tried to use tools/r2-sparse-partition-patch.py.
That was rejected because the helper still encodes pre-v0.33 fixed product_b
extents. After system_b growth, product_b begins at super sector 15104000 in
the v0.35.1 lpdump, not the helper's old sector 17021888. The builder was
therefore corrected to the v0.35.1-style lpunpack/lpmake path.
```

