## 2026-06-20 v0.37a TextBoom live system-base candidate

Scope:

```text
variant:
  v0.37a-textboom-live-system-base
source baseline:
  v0.36.1-smartisax-shell-debloat-arsc-align
boundary:
  offline image build and verification only
  no flash
  no reboot
  no package mutation
  no /data cleanup
purpose:
  prepare the Big Bang/TextBoom OCR modernization route without first changing
  OCR behavior or touching TextBoom AndroidManifest.xml
```

Design decision:

```text
Directly switching TextBoom from CsOcr to SmashOcr is not safe yet because both
the stock and live TextBoom APKs contain SmashOcr Java/SWIG code and
assets/tt_general_ocr_v1.0.model, but no libsmash_ocr_lib.so. Instantiating
SmashOcr would hit System.loadLibrary("smash_ocr_lib") and likely crash.

TextBoom's live APK is v1/JAR-signed and has no APK Signature Scheme v2/v3
block. Prior v0.26a manifest-edit evidence proved that changing
AndroidManifest.xml can make PackageManager certificate collection fail on this
device. Therefore v0.37a does not bump versionCode and does not edit
AndroidManifest.xml. It copies the live v3.2.2 APK byte-for-byte into
/system/app/TextBoom and bumps only /system/app/TextBoom plus /system/app mtimes.
```

Inputs:

```text
live TextBoom APK:
  apks/textboom-live/TextBoom-live-v3.2.2-base.apk
  sha256=52df3deb5315baf41b9f5476a122ce9782fa58f74076d1d4a9c060c9c506873c
package:
  com.smartisanos.textboom
version:
  versionCode=104
  versionName=3.2.2
signature:
  v1/JAR verified
  APK Sig Block 42 absent
```

Follow-up root/PM probes:

```text

result:
  both hashes are 52df3deb5315baf41b9f5476a122ce9782fa58f74076d1d4a9c060c9c506873c

  tools/r2-root.sh cmd 'cmd package uninstall-system-updates com.smartisanos.textboom; pm path com.smartisanos.textboom'
result:
  same failure; active path remained /data/app

ordinary uninstall retry:
  pm uninstall -k com.smartisanos.textboom
result:
  Failure [DELETE_FAILED_INTERNAL_ERROR]
  active path remained /data/app

packages.xml state:
  active package:
    <package name="com.smartisanos.textboom" codePath="/data/app/com.smartisanos.textboom-Mqq5N4_nzUzyyOKZ2Y6jIw==" ... version="104" userId="10148" isOrphaned="true">
  disabled system base:
    <updated-package name="com.smartisanos.textboom" codePath="/system/app/TextBoom" ... version="104" ... userId="10148">

package_cache state:
  /data/system/package_cache/.../TextBoom-16
  /data/system/package_cache/.../com.smartisanos.textboom-Mqq5N4_nzUzyyOKZ2Y6jIw==-0
```

Source interpretation:

```text
PackageManagerService.addForInitLI:
  isSystemPkgBetter requires:
    scanSystemPartition
    isSystemPkgUpdated
    newPkgChangedPaths
    newPkgVersionGreater
  TextBoom v0.37a has changed paths but equal versionCode 104, so PackageManager
  will not automatically prefer /system/app/TextBoom on boot scan.

PackageManagerShellCommand.uninstallSystemUpdates:
  calls PackageInstaller uninstall for updated-system packages and only prints
  "Couldn't uninstall package" on nonzero status.

PackageManagerServiceSmtEx:
  mCanUninstalledSystemApp includes com.smartisanos.textboom, but
  isSystemAppCanDeleted() explicitly requires !isUpdatedSystemApp(ps).
  The current active TextBoom is SYSTEM + UPDATED_SYSTEM_APP, so the Smartisan
  system-app delete path does not treat it as an ordinary deletable system app.

dumpsys package:
  active /data package has apkSigningVersion=1 and a v1 signature.
  hidden /system base is recorded as apkSigningVersion=0/signatures=[] in the
  disabled-system package state even though the APK file itself is v1/JAR
  verified. This makes the stock updated-system rollback path fragile here.
```

Boundary after this attempt:

```text
v0.37a remains booted and stable, but TextBoom is not yet active from the ROM
system base. The current `tools/r2-clean-v0.37a-textboom-data.sh` script was
fixed after this run so future --apply attempts exit nonzero when `pm path`
still points at /data/app.

Do not run direct /data/app deletion, packages.xml surgery, or rebooting
package-cache repair without a new explicit confirmation. A mere package_cache
clear is unlikely to complete the switch because the promoted system base and
the /data updated-system package have the same versionCode.
```

## 2026-06-20 v0.37b TextBoom system libs/deodex candidate

Variant:

```text
v0.37b-textboom-live-system-libs-deodex
```

Reason:

```text
The v0.37a system base is byte-identical to the live TextBoom v3.2.2 APK, but
the active updated-system /data app also contains extracted 32-bit native libs
under /data/app/.../lib/arm. The v0.37a system tree had only an empty
/system/app/TextBoom/lib/arm64 directory and stale stock oat/vdex under
/system/app/TextBoom/oat/arm64. A hard PackageManager shadow repair on v0.37a
could therefore fall back to a system APK without the native library layout it
expects, and with stale stock oat still present.

v0.37b keeps the TextBoom APK byte-identical, adds the 13 armeabi-v7a native
libraries from that APK into /system/app/TextBoom/lib/arm, removes
/system/app/TextBoom/oat, bumps TextBoom and /system/app mtimes, and rebuilds
system_b FEC roots=2.
```

Builder and verifier:

```text
tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.37b-textboom-live-system-libs-deodex.sh
tools/r2-verify-v0.37b-textboom-live-system-libs-deodex.sh
```

Build command:

```text
tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.37b-textboom-live-system-libs-deodex.sh
```

## 2026-06-20 - TextBoom/Sidebar OCR backend map

Focused static backend map:

```text
command:
  tools/r2-textboom-ocr-backend-map.py
report:
  docs/research/textboom-ocr-backend-map.md
machine artifacts:
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/textboom-ocr-backend-map.tsv
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ocr-backend-map/textboom-ocr-backend-map.json
result:
  TEXTBOOM_OCR_BACKEND_MAP_OFFLINE_PASS
evidence:
  TextBoom framework trigger is PressGestureDetector -> OcrFloatViewService
  OcrFloatViewService/VoiceUtils launches BoomOcrActivity through ACTION_BOOM_IMAGE
  BoomOcrActivity hard-codes new CsOcr in initView
  BoomAccessOcrActivity hard-codes new CsOcr but also has an online OcrThread branch
  CsOcr implements IOcrApi and delegates to Intsig/CamScanner CSOpenAPI ACTION_OCR
  SmashOcr is present as an unused IOcrApi implementation
  current live TextBoom APK contains assets/tt_general_ocr_v1.0.model
  current live TextBoom APK does not contain libsmash_ocr_lib.so
  Sidebar/One Step font OCR is a separate IdentifyFontView -> FontUtils
  -> BoomFontActivity -> OCRhelper -> CSOpenAPI path
  Sidebar font lookup has a second remote dependency:
  FontHelper uploads glyph images/text to qiuziti.com after OCR
decision:
  do not blind-switch TextBoom from CsOcr to SmashOcr
  retire the SmashOcr route instead of repairing the missing native loader
  retire Sidebar/One Step font OCR instead of migrating it to PP-OCR
  use CsOcr as temporary baseline for TextBoom only
  prototype a local IOcrApi-compatible PP-OCR benchmark harness first
  benchmark Baidu/PaddleOCR PP-OCRv6 tiny/small on real R2 screenshots before
  integrating any local OCR engine into the ROM
```

## 2026-06-20 - OCR full-deletion and PP-OCR replacement plan

User correction:

```text
Sidebar/One Step font OCR should not merely be disabled. The related code
should be fully removed. CamScanner-related OCR code should also be removed
while PP-OCR becomes the replacement route for TextBoom image OCR.
```

Offline planner and tests:

```text
commands:
  python3 -m unittest tests.test_r2_ocr_ppocr_replacement_plan
  python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-ocr-ppocr-replacement-plan.py tests/test_r2_ocr_ppocr_replacement_plan.py
  python3 tools/r2-ocr-ppocr-replacement-plan.py
result:
  tests PASS, 7 tests
  OCR_PPOCR_REPLACEMENT_PLAN_OFFLINE_PASS
report:
  docs/research/ocr-ppocr-replacement-plan.md
machine artifacts:
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/ocr-ppocr-replacement-plan.tsv
  hard-rom/inspect/ocr-ppocr-replacement-plan/ocr-ppocr-replacement-plan.json
```

Corrected v0.38 boundary:

```text
v0.38-sidebar-font-ocr-disabled is a stable launch/behavior stop, not a
complete code deletion. It proves Sidebar/One Step can remain boot-stable and
usable after ACTION_BOOM_FONT no longer resolves, but decoded evidence still
contains the retired font OCR and CamScanner-related code.
```

Pending deletion gates found by the planner:

```text
Sidebar:
  BoomFontActivity manifest declaration and ocr_key metadata remain
  com/smartisanos/sidebar/open/font backend classes remain
  Sidebar-local com/intsig/csopen SDK copy remains
  ToolButtonManager/ToolButtonAdapter still have type=1 reachability

TextBoom:
  BoomOcrActivity and BoomAccessOcrActivity still route through CsOcr
  TextBoom-local com/intsig/csopen SDK copy remains
  TextBoom manifest ocr_key metadata remains
  BoomAccessOcrActivity has a separate online OcrThread branch that needs an
  explicit local-only or keep-with-reason decision
```

PP-OCR route decision:

```text
current external facts:
  PaddleOCR README records a 2026-06-11 PaddleOCR 3.7.0 release and PP-OCRv6
  tiny/small/medium tiers
  PP-OCRv6 is described by the official README as a single model supporting
  50 languages
  the official Android demo remains a PaddleLite runtime reference, not a
  drop-in R2 ROM integration
route:
  v0.39 should be a Sidebar APK-only code-deletion candidate first
  TextBoom PP-OCR should be benchmarked through a local harness before ROM
  integration
  the TextBoom replacement should keep IOcrApi as the compatibility boundary,
  move result normalization/OcrInfo conversion into pure tested functions, and
  delete CsOcr/Intsig in the same accepted candidate so there is no silent
  fallback to CamScanner
flash gate:
  no flash authorized by this planning step
```

## 2026-06-20 - PP-OCR to TextBoom pure mapping boundary

User correction:

```text
字体 OCR is not merely to be disabled; the related code should be fully
removed. CamScanner-related TextBoom OCR code should also be fully removed, but
only in the same candidate that replaces it with PP-OCR so TextBoom does not
silently lose image OCR capability.
```

Implemented local boundary:

```text
tool:
  tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-mapping.py
tests:
  tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_mapping.py
role:
  pure PP-OCR output -> TextBoom-compatible OCR line mapping
inputs handled:
  classic PaddleOCR nested line-pair shape
  dictionary-style rec_texts/rec_scores/dt_polys shape
  flat 8-number quadrilateral boxes
output:
  TextBoom-compatible rows with text, rect(left/top/right/bottom), and score
behavior:
  trims text and removes carriage returns like legacy CsOcr
  converts quadrilateral boxes to axis-aligned rectangles
  clamps rectangles to bitmap bounds when provided
  filters empty and low-confidence lines
  sorts rows in reading order
boundary:
  no Android dependency
  no APK dependency
  no native runtime dependency
  no filesystem or device access
```

Validation:

```text
commands:
  python3 -m unittest tests.test_r2_textboom_ppocr_mapping
  python3 -m unittest tests.test_r2_ocr_ppocr_replacement_plan
  python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-mapping.py tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_mapping.py
result:
  PP-OCR mapping tests PASS, 6 tests
  OCR replacement plan tests PASS, 7 tests
```

Next PP-OCR gate:

```text
Create the native/runtime benchmark harness or adapter around this mapping
layer. Only after PP-OCR returns acceptable latency, memory, recognition
quality, and coordinate quality should TextBoom's CsOcr, TextBoom-local
com/intsig/csopen SDK copy, ocr_key metadata, and BoomAccessOcrActivity online
OcrThread branch be removed from a ROM candidate.
```

## 2026-06-20 - TextBoom PP-OCR offline benchmark harness

Purpose:

```text
Continue the PP-OCR replacement route after v0.39 without touching TextBoom APK
or flashing a new ROM. The immediate need is a reusable acceptance gate for the
future v0.40 TextBoom/CamScanner replacement: saved CsOcr baseline output and
saved PP-OCRv6 tiny/small output must be scored against the same labeled R2
screenshot corpus before any TextBoom code is repacked.
```

Implemented:

```text
tool:
  tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-benchmark.py
  tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-corpus-template.py
tests:
  tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_benchmark.py
  tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_corpus_template.py
role:
  offline saved-result benchmark harness
  offline screenshot corpus template generator
inputs:
  labeled corpus JSON with sample id, optional image_size, and expected text/rect lines
  prediction JSON from CsOcr baseline capture or PP-OCR runtime capture
metrics:
  line recall
  character error rate
  matched rectangle IoU
  optional p50/p95 latency
  optional peak PSS memory
outputs:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-benchmark/<label>.json
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-benchmark/<label>.md
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-benchmark/<label>.tsv
optional docs/manifest outputs:
  --docs-md
  --manifest-tsv
corpus template:
  reads local PNG/JPEG screenshots
  writes hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-corpus/corpus-template.json by default
  preserves already-filled expected labels with --preserve-existing
generated seed template:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-corpus/v0.39-existing-screens-corpus-template.json
  contains four existing v0.39 verification screenshots with image_size populated
  expected labels are intentionally empty and must be manually filled before benchmark use
```

Validation:

```text
commands:
  python3 -m unittest tests.test_r2_textboom_ppocr_mapping tests.test_r2_textboom_ppocr_benchmark tests.test_r2_textboom_ppocr_corpus_template tests.test_r2_ocr_ppocr_replacement_plan
  python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-mapping.py tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-benchmark.py tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-corpus-template.py tools/r2-ocr-ppocr-replacement-plan.py tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_mapping.py tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_benchmark.py tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_corpus_template.py tests/test_r2_ocr_ppocr_replacement_plan.py
result:
  21 OCR-related unit tests PASS
  py_compile PASS
```

Boundary:

```text
This harness does not run PP-OCR.
The corpus-template generator does not capture from a device.
It does not patch TextBoom.
It does not build or flash a ROM image.
It does not authorize deletion of TextBoom CsOcr, TextBoom com/intsig/csopen,
TextBoom ocr_key, or BoomAccessOcrActivity's online OCR branch by itself.

Next live-facing step:
  capture a small labeled R2 screenshot corpus and saved baseline predictions,
  then run PP-OCRv6 tiny/small predictions through the same harness before
  designing the v0.40 TextBoom APK replacement candidate.
```

## 2026-06-20 - TextBoom PP-OCR live capture gate: unlocked CsOcr baseline

Purpose:

```text
Collect the first real v0.39 TextBoom image-OCR baseline while the device is
unlocked. This validates the current CsOcr/CamScanner route before replacing it
with a local PP-OCR backend.
```

Device state:

```text
serial=bb12d264
slot=_b
boot=1
build=8.5.3-202207181710-user-drw
keyguard=false at capture start
foreground=com.smartisax.browser/.ShellActivity before BOOM_IMAGE
```

Live artifacts:

```text
capture dir:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-live-capture/20260620-230945-unlocked-boom-image/
script evidence:
  capture.txt
crop surface:
  02-after-start-3s.png
  02-after-start-3s.xml
result page:
  05-after-tap-10s.png
  05-after-tap-10s.xml
  05-after-tap-10s.logcat.txt

  device-files/imageboom.jpg
  /sdcard/.boom/imageboom.jpg
  size=154521 bytes
  dimensions=1080x773

  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-corpus/v0.39-live-imageboom-corpus-template.json
UI-result baseline:
  textboom-ui-result-baseline.json
```

Observed current route:

```text
ACTION:
  smartisanos.intent.action.BOOM_IMAGE
RESOLVES:
  com.smartisanos.textboom/.ocr.BoomOcrActivity
USER FLOW:
  crop screen title="框选需要识别的区域"
  user tapped bottom-right "识别文字"
OCR INPUT:
  BoomOcrActivity startOcr bitmap width=1080 bitmap height=773
BACKEND:
  com.smartisanos.textboom.ocr.CsOcr
  com.intsig.camscanner.ACTION_OCR
  com.intsig.camscanner/.openapi.OCROpenApiActivity
RESULT:
  BoomOcrActivity onActivityResult resultCode=-1
  BoomOcrActivity startBoomActivity
  TextBoom result page title="大爆炸"
  total_size_text="共 223 字"
  keyguard=false during result capture
```

Timing from logcat:

```text
23:10:40.851  BoomOcrActivity: startOcrCropped bitmap is null =false
23:10:40.863  BoomOcrActivity: startOcr bitmap width = 1080 bitmap height = 773
23:10:41.061  ActivityTaskManager START com.intsig.camscanner.ACTION_OCR
23:10:42.045  BoomOcrActivity: onActivityResult: resultCode = -1, data is null false
23:10:42.061  BoomOcrActivity: startBoomActivity
```

Interpretation:

```text
The legacy route is alive and functional on v0.39:
TextBoom -> CsOcr -> CamScanner OpenAPI -> TextBoom result page.

Measured from TextBoom startOcr log to onActivityResult, the user-visible OCR
backend round trip was roughly 1.2 seconds on this sample. The result page then
feeds TextBoom's existing text-participle provider.

This baseline is not raw CamScanner RESPONSE_DATA. The new
tools/r2-textboom-ui-result-baseline.py records what TextBoom rendered from the
result UI so future PP-OCR builds can compare user-visible output. Raw line-level
CSOcrResult capture still requires an instrumented TextBoom build or another
hook before deleting com/intsig/csopen code.
```

Validation:

```text
commands:
  python3 -m unittest tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_mapping.py tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_benchmark.py tests/test_r2_textboom_ppocr_corpus_template.py tests/test_r2_textboom_ui_result_baseline.py
  python3 -m py_compile tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-mapping.py tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-benchmark.py tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-corpus-template.py tools/r2-textboom-ui-result-baseline.py
result:
  16 OCR-related unit tests PASS
  py_compile PASS
```

Non-result artifact:

```text
06-after-result-scroll.png/xml/focus were captured after the screen had locked.
They show Keyguard, not TextBoom, and must not be used as OCR baseline data.
```

Next step:

```text
Stay offline unless more live samples are needed. The next engineering step is a
no-ROM PP-OCR runtime candidate/benchmark harness that consumes imageboom.jpg and
emits the existing tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-mapping.py shape. Do not remove
CsOcr/CamScanner from TextBoom until PP-OCR passes quality, latency, and memory
gates on several real R2 samples.
```

```text
loop shortens benchmark APK iteration.
```

Purpose:

```text
Create the first no-ROM, no-TextBoom-modification Android runtime harness for
the PP-OCR replacement route. The first APK is deliberately a stub backend: it
proves Android 11 packaging, app launch, image-file probing, saved prediction
JSON shape, latency field, and PSS memory field before Paddle Lite libraries or
PP-OCR model files are vendored.
```

```text
No phone operation was performed.
No flash, reboot, erase, uninstall, data cleanup, or ROM image mutation was
performed.

This APK does not run Paddle Lite or PP-OCR yet. It emits `ppocr: []` with
image metadata and runtime metrics. The next gate is live installation and stub
JSON collection on R2, then adding PP-OCRv6 tiny runtime/model assets to this
same standalone harness.

The live smoke helper was prepared but not executed in this step. It must be
run only after explicit user approval because it installs the benchmark APK and
writes app-specific external files on the live device.
```

## 2026-06-21 - TextBoom PP-OCR standalone benchmark native Paddle Lite smoke

Purpose:

```text
Replace the standalone OCR bench stub with a real Android-native Paddle Lite
pipeline, run PP-OCR on the same imageboom.jpg sample used for the current
CsOcr/TextBoom baseline, and record quality, latency, and memory evidence before
any TextBoom ROM integration.
```

Checkpoint commit before runtime work:

```text
commit:
  6934fd6 chore: checkpoint hardrom tooling and ppocr bench
validation before commit:
  python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
  python3 -m py_compile tools/*.py tests/*.py
  git diff --cached --check
result:
  PASS
```

Runtime build:

```text
builder:
  tools/r2-build-textboom-ppocr-bench-apk.sh
app:
  apps/TextBoomPpOcrBench/
package:
  com.smartisax.ocrbench
apk:
  hard-rom/build/apk/TextBoomPpOcrBench.apk
sha256:
  f9765fe7cd305d2d67916fd4f1bbd0189fa590d646e967710f9e58ea3b0c9d4f
native runtime:
  libpaddle_light_api_shared.so
  libsmartisax_ppocr_bench.so
  libc++_shared.so
model:
  PP-OCR mobile v2 slim det/cls/rec .nb files from the official
  Paddle-Lite demo asset set
```

PP-OCRv6 boundary:

```text
PP-OCRv6 tiny/small inference packages were inspected first, but their current
Paddle 3/PIR model shape is inference.json + inference.pdiparams rather than
legacy inference.pdmodel + inference.pdiparams. The local Paddle Lite v2.14 opt
binary can print model ops, but conversion to a runnable naive_buffer model
currently fails with a BlocksSize() check. Therefore this live runtime gate uses
the official PP-OCR mobile v2 slim .nb assets to prove the native runtime path
on R2 before solving the PP-OCRv6 conversion gap.
```

Live run:

```text

output:
  PASS_PPOCR_BENCH_LIVE_SMOKE
evidence:

  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-bench-live/20260621-ppocr-v210-heap-det-script-pass2/summary.txt
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-bench-live/20260621-ppocr-v210-heap-det-script-pass2/last-result.json
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-bench-live/20260621-ppocr-v210-heap-det-script-pass2/csocr-ppocr-comparison.md
boundary:
  writes app-specific external files and launches an already-installed bench
  APK only; no flash, reboot, erase, uninstall, data cleanup, TextBoom mutation,
  ROM mutation, or system package mutation.
```

Result:

```text

  PP_OCR_READY

  1080x773

  85c8d407caa2df8a0f644182f3bc2fb9c0caebcaaecb4c531de344bcde645f6a
```

Comparison boundary:

```text
The existing CsOcr baseline is a TextBoom UI extraction, not raw CsOcr
RESPONSE_DATA. It is useful for visible text quality comparison and shows better
punctuation/case on the visible user-agent text, but it cannot yet provide
direct CsOcr latency or memory numbers.

Current verdict:
  real native Paddle Lite PP-OCR runtime on R2 is PASS;
  PP-OCR v2 mobile slim quality is PARTIAL for technical text;
  final replacement decision needs raw CsOcr response/timing/memory capture and
  either PP-OCRv6 conversion or post-processing/model tuning.
```

## 2026-06-21 - PP-OCR route correction to official ppocr-android

Purpose:

```text
Record the side-thread route correction after checking the current official
PaddleOCR Android deployment docs and source tree. The formal TextBoom
replacement route is no longer a hand-rolled PP-OCRv6 tiny ONNX pipeline.
It is official deploy/ppocr-android/ppocr-sdk + PP-OCRv6 small + native
ONNX Runtime Android CPU inference.
```

Official sources:

```text
docs:
  https://www.paddleocr.ai/main/en/version3.x/inference_deployment/cross_platform/android_deployment.html
source:
  https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR/tree/main/deploy/ppocr-android
```

Local intake:

```text
script:
  tools/r2-fetch-official-ppocr-android.sh
result:
  PASS_OFFICIAL_PPOCR_ANDROID_INTAKE
local source:
  third_party/_downloads/paddleocr-ppocr-android/PaddleOCR/deploy/ppocr-android/
commit:
  ef346e0b402934477489001a4d253a20dbeb72a5
manifest:
  third_party/_downloads/paddleocr-ppocr-android/ppocr-android-manifest.txt
```

Route correction:

```text
main candidate:
  PP-OCRv6_small_det.onnx + PP-OCRv6_small_rec.onnx
runtime:
  native ONNX Runtime Android, CPU-resident service shape
official dependency baseline:
  onnxruntime-android 1.21.1
  OpenCV 4.5.3
  minSdk 26
official model layout:
  models/det/inference.onnx
  models/rec/inference.onnx
  models/rec/inference.yml
API output:
  line-level text, confidence, box points, and timing breakdowns
TextBoom-specific work still required:
  character/click-block coordinates, Chinese segmentation, URL/phone/address
  recognition, OcrInfo mapping, and CsOcr baseline comparison
```

Boundary:

```text
The already live-proven PP-OCRv6 tiny ONNX smoke remains a compatibility proof
for model conversion and ORT Android/WebView runtime loading. Tiny is now only a
speed/power fallback. WebView/ORT Web remains a Smartisax Shell experiment, not
the production TextBoom replacement dependency.

The old deploy/android_demo PaddleLite v2.10 path remains historical reference
only. It should not drive the PP-OCRv6 Android implementation.
```

Next gate:

```text
Build a new no-ROM benchmark harness around official ppocr-sdk first. Reuse the
official preprocessing, DB postprocess, crop, CTC decode, model-directory, and
config handling. Run PP-OCRv6 small on imageboom.jpg and the R2 screenshot
corpus, capture raw CsOcr response/timing/memory for the same inputs, then add
pure TextBoom OcrInfo mapping tests before any TextBoom APK or ROM mutation.
```

## 2026-06-21 - Official PP-OCRv6 small benchmark APK built offline

Purpose:

```text
Convert the corrected TextBoom PP-OCR route into a no-ROM benchmark APK before
touching TextBoom or deleting its CsOcr/CamScanner image-OCR path. This APK
reuses the official deploy/ppocr-android/ppocr-sdk implementation, packages
PP-OCRv6 small in the official asset layout, and records line text/confidence/
box/timing/memory JSON for later comparison with the CsOcr baseline.
```

Inputs:

```text
official ppocr-android source:
  third_party/_downloads/paddleocr-ppocr-android/PaddleOCR/deploy/ppocr-android/
official source commit:
  ef346e0b402934477489001a4d253a20dbeb72a5
models:
  hard-rom/build/ppocr-runtime/onnx/PP-OCRv6_small_det/model.onnx
  hard-rom/build/ppocr-runtime/onnx/PP-OCRv6_small_rec/model.onnx
  hard-rom/build/ppocr-runtime/onnx/PP-OCRv6_small_rec/inference.yml
```

Local tooling fix:

```text
script:
  tools/r2-fetch-local-jdk17.sh
installed JDK:
  third_party/_downloads/jdk/temurin-17/Contents/Home
version:
  Temurin 17.0.19+10
reason:
  Homebrew OpenJDK 26.0.1 is too new for this Gradle/Kotlin Android build;
  the Kotlin Gradle script compiler rejects Java version string 26.0.1.
build script repository order:
  Aliyun Google/Gradle/Public Maven mirrors first, then google/mavenCentral/
  gradlePluginPortal fallback, because Java TLS through the current dl.google
  proxy path reports `SSLv2Hello is not enabled`.
```

Build:

```text
app:
  apps/TextBoomPpOcrOfficialBench/
builder:
  tools/r2-build-textboom-ppocr-official-bench-apk.sh
live helper:
  tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-official-bench-live-smoke.sh
output:
  hard-rom/build/apk/TextBoomPpOcrOfficialBench.apk
package:
  com.smartisax.ocrbench.officialbench
version:
  0.1.0 / 1
runtime:
  onnxruntime-android 1.21.1
  OpenCV 4.5.3
ABI:
  arm64-v8a
size:
  41M
sha256:
  b67b80caa4cbd6f338942ea0addb00b80f82079f721eb9f86bba0b83241fab74
manifest:
  hard-rom/build/apk/TextBoomPpOcrOfficialBench.SHA256SUMS.txt
```

```text
apksigner:

```


## 2026-06-21 - Official PP-OCRv6 small benchmark live smoke and OpenCV fix

Purpose:

```text
Run the standalone official PP-OCRv6 small benchmark APK on the live R2 before
any TextBoom APK/ROM mutation, then fix the first runtime blocker while keeping
the official ppocr-sdk pipeline and ORT Android 1.21.1 route intact.
```

First live smoke:

```text
command:
  RUN_ID=20260621-ppocr-official-small-live-smoke INSTALL_MODE=pm \
    ./tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-official-bench-live-smoke.sh
result:
  OCR_ERROR
evidence:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-official-bench-live/20260621-ppocr-official-small-live-smoke/
sample:
  imageboom.jpg, 1080x773
sha256:
  85c8d407caa2df8a0f644182f3bc2fb9c0caebcaaecb4c531de344bcde645f6a
runtime error:
  IllegalStateException: Failed to initialize OpenCV native library
root cause:
  QuickBird OpenCV 4.5.3 packaged libopencv_java4.so references
  __sfp_handle_exceptions, which is not resolvable on the R2 Android 11 runtime.
```

Fix:

```text
changed files:
  tools/r2-build-textboom-ppocr-official-bench-apk.sh
  apps/TextBoomPpOcrOfficialBench/src/main/java/com/smartisax/ocrbench/officialbench/MainActivity.kt
change:
  Keep onnxruntime-android 1.21.1 and official ppocr-sdk source, but replace
  com.quickbirdstudios:opencv:4.5.3 with official org.opencv:opencv:4.9.0.
version:
  0.1.1 / 2
apk:
  hard-rom/build/apk/TextBoomPpOcrOfficialBench.apk
sha256:
  eacf4f3f6e25b806273d686dd0f49b0ed7f3d10f8124ce8bab596dba8df46f3b
packaging:
  resources.arsc STORED and 4-byte aligned
native ABI check:
  packaged official libopencv_java4.so no longer exposes the
  __sfp_handle_exceptions unresolved-symbol blocker seen in QuickBird 4.5.3.
```

Successful live smoke:

```text
command:
  RUN_ID=20260621-ppocr-official-small-opencv490-live-smoke INSTALL_MODE=pm \
    ./tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-official-bench-live-smoke.sh
result:
  PASS_PPOCR_OFFICIAL_BENCH_LIVE_SMOKE
evidence:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-official-bench-live/20260621-ppocr-official-small-opencv490-live-smoke/
device:
  SMARTISAN DT2002C / darwin, Android 11, slot _b
engine:
  official-paddleocr-ppocr-android 0.1.1
  onnxruntime-android 1.21.1
  OpenCV 4.9.0 official AAR
model:
  PP-OCRv6 small det + rec, official asset layout
sample:
  imageboom.jpg, 1080x773
sha256:
  85c8d407caa2df8a0f644182f3bc2fb9c0caebcaaecb4c531de344bcde645f6a
metrics:
  line_count=14
  latency_ms=940
  wall_elapsed_ms=1306
  cold_load_ms=313
  det_ms=400
  rec_ms=528
  peak_pss_kb=77247
recognized lines:
  Runtime
  WebView
  Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11;
  Build/RKQ1.201217.002; wv)
  AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like
  Gecko) Version/4.0
  Chrome/150.0.7871.28 Mobile
  Safari/537.36
  WebGPU
  available
  WebGL2
  available
  Storage
  localStorage ready
boundary:
  This was a standalone benchmark APK install/run and app-specific file write
  on /data. No ROM image, TextBoom APK, CamScanner/CsOcr code, flash, reboot,
  erase, or package data cleanup was performed.
```

Next gate:

```text
Collect the CsOcr/CamScanner baseline on the same imageboom.jpg and screenshot
corpus, then compare quality, latency, and memory before designing the
TextBoom adapter/OcrInfo mapping layer.
```

```text
app-specific external files directory.
```

Standalone CsOcr/CamScanner raw baseline probe:

```text
command:
  RUN_ID=20260621-csocr-corpus-standalone-v1 SKIP_INSTALL=1 \
    ./tools/r2-textboom-csocr-baseline-live.sh <same six samples>
result:
  FAIL_CSOCR_BASELINE_LIVE
evidence:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-csocr-baseline-live/20260621-csocr-corpus-standalone-v1/
observed:
  all six samples returned CSOCR_RESULT_CODE_1
  response_code=4003
  raw_response_size=0
  parsed line_count=0
memory:
  benchmark app PSS about 15-16 MB
  CamScanner total PSS about 154-157 MB
interpretation:
  The standalone caller cannot recover raw CamScanner RESPONSE_DATA. Treat this
  as a caller/package/signature/key-bound OpenAPI rejection, not as an OCR
  quality score. If exact raw CsOcr parity is still required, the next route is
  TextBoom-internal instrumentation before replacing CsOcr.
```

Comparison report:

```text
command:
  python3 tools/r2-textboom-ocr-compare-report.py \
    --ppocr hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-official-corpus-live/20260621-ppocr-official-small-corpus-v1/ppocr-official-corpus-results.json \
    --csocr hard-rom/inspect/textboom-csocr-baseline-live/20260621-csocr-corpus-standalone-v1/csocr-baseline-results.json \
    --ui-baseline hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-live-capture/20260620-230945-unlocked-boom-image/textboom-ui-result-baseline.json \
    --ui-baseline hard-rom/inspect/textboom-ppocr-live-capture/20260620-230945-unlocked-boom-image/textboom-ui-result-scroll-baseline.json \
    --label 20260621-v039-ppocr-small-vs-csocr-standalone \
    --docs-md docs/research/textboom-ocr-baseline-comparison.md \
    --manifest-tsv reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/manifest/textboom-ocr-baseline-comparison.tsv
result:
  TEXTBOOM_OCR_BASELINE_COMPARE_PARTIAL
reason:
  PP-OCR has complete line/latency/memory output, while raw CsOcr is blocked.
imageboom visible UI note:
  TextBoom's saved UI baseline is only the visible top portion of the result
  page. Against the PP-OCR full-image output, full CER is 0.8319 because the
  candidate includes additional lower lines, but prefix CER is 0.00885 for the
  visible top text.
```

Adapter/OcrInfo design:

```text
doc:
  docs/research/textboom-ppocr-adapter-design.md
status:
  READY_FOR_TEXTBOOM_ADAPTER_PROTOTYPE
pure mapping:
  tools/r2-textboom-ppocr-mapping.py now emits both {text, rect, score} and
  OcrInfo-shaped {mText, mRect, score} rows.
next gate:
  Build a TextBoom LocalPpOcrApi no-op adapter candidate first. This separates
  TextBoom APK/package-cache/signature risk from the full PP-OCR model/runtime
  integration risk.
```

Scope:

```text
goal:

route:

device impact:

  performed in this step.
```

```text
  The no-op adapter path returns to the normal Big Bang page with empty/no
  result behavior. Captured logs have no CSOpenApi/CsOcr/CamScanner OCR call
  marker and no TextBoom fatal crash marker.
offline code cross-check:
  decoded v0.40 TextBoom smali shows BoomOcrActivity creates
  com.smartisanos.textboom.ocr.LocalPpOcrApi at initView, while retained
  legacy CsOcr classes remain present for this no-op gate.
```

Conclusion:

```text
result:
  v0.40 is now the live B-slot TextBoom PP-OCR no-op adapter gate.
safe next step:
  Build the real PP-OCR runtime candidate behind LocalPpOcrApi using the
  official PP-OCRv6 small ONNX + Android runtime route.
deletion boundary:
  Do not remove CsOcr, TextBoom-local Intsig classes, or ocr_key until the
  real local PP-OCR runtime passes live image OCR quality, latency, memory, and
  stability checks.
rollback:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.4-debloat-exact-current.sparse.img
```

## 2026-06-21 - v0.41 TextBoom PP-OCR runtime adapter live flash and ABI failure

Scope:

```text
variant:
  v0.41-textboom-ppocr-runtime-adapter
goal:
  Flash the first real TextBoom PP-OCR runtime adapter to B slot, verify boot
  and package state, then exercise BOOM_TEXT and BOOM_IMAGE before deleting any
  legacy CsOcr/Intsig/ocr_key code.
important correction:
  The desktop state during verification was user-assisted. The user manually
  swiped past keyguard into the desktop; this must not be counted as an
  automatic unlock or agent-completed unlock step.
```

```text
    BoomOcrActivity: startOcr bitmap width = 1080 bitmap height = 773
    OpenCVUtils: Failed to initialize OpenCV: dlopen failed: library "libopencv_java4.so" not found

  TOTAL PSS about 145694 KB
  Native Heap about 52924 KB

result:
  BOOM_TEXT passes
  BOOM_IMAGE reaches the local PP-OCR adapter, but runtime init fails before
  inference because the needed native library is not available to the running
  process ABI
```

ABI diagnosis:

```text
live PackageManager:
  primaryCpuAbi=armeabi-v7a
  secondaryCpuAbi=null
live process:
  /system/bin/app_process32
device ABI props:
  ro.zygote=zygote64_32
  ro.product.cpu.abilist=arm64-v8a,armeabi-v7a,armeabi
  ro.product.cpu.abilist32=armeabi-v7a,armeabi
  ro.product.cpu.abilist64=arm64-v8a
v0.41 image state:
  existing /system/app/TextBoom/lib/arm retained from v0.37b
  new ORT/OpenCV runtime libs were written only under /system/app/TextBoom/lib/arm64
AAR evidence:
  onnxruntime-android 1.21.1 contains armeabi-v7a libonnxruntime.so and libonnxruntime4j_jni.so
  OpenCV 4.9.0 AAR contains armeabi-v7a libopencv_java4.so and libc++_shared.so
dependency check:
  32-bit libopencv_java4.so needs libc++_shared.so plus platform libs
  32-bit libonnxruntime.so needs only platform libs
  32-bit libonnxruntime4j_jni.so needs libonnxruntime.so plus platform libs
```

Conclusion:

```text
result:
  v0.41 is flashed and boot/package/BOOM_TEXT verified, but is not a passing
  image-OCR runtime gate.
root cause:
  TextBoom is a 32-bit app on this ROM, so Java/OpenCV/ORT native loading looks
  under the 32-bit app library path. v0.41 only added the new runtime libraries
  under /system/app/TextBoom/lib/arm64.
next candidate:
  v0.41.1-textboom-ppocr-runtime-arm32-libs
planned patch:
  add armeabi-v7a libopencv_java4.so, libonnxruntime.so, and
  libonnxruntime4j_jni.so under /system/app/TextBoom/lib/arm
  keep the existing 32-bit TextBoom libc++_shared.so first to minimize change
  keep the v0.41 TextBoom APK hash stable
  extend offline/live verifiers to assert primaryCpuAbi=armeabi-v7a and the
  32-bit runtime files
deletion boundary:
  Do not delete CsOcr, TextBoom-local Intsig classes, or ocr_key until a
  successor real local PP-OCR runtime passes live image OCR quality, latency,
  memory, and stability.
rollback:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.4-debloat-exact-current.sparse.img
```

```text
approval.
```

```text
v0.41.1 line passes this broader quality, latency, memory, and stability gate.
```

Live TextBoom BOOM_IMAGE regression:

```text

  RUN_ID=20260621-v0411-live-textboom-ocr-regression-v2 \
    tools/r2-textboom-live-ocr-regression.sh
boundary:

  uninstall, package data cleanup, or ROM mutation

```

Live OCR result:

```text
case                  launch activity                                  count text   wall ms   TextBoom total PSS KB
smartisax_home         com.smartisanos.textboom/.ocr.BoomOcrActivity    共 226 字     10002    192014
settings_main          com.smartisanos.textboom/.ocr.BoomOcrActivity    共 33 字       9974    243787
textboom_app_details   com.smartisanos.textboom/.ocr.BoomOcrActivity    共 40 字      10009    246357
```

Stability:

```text
TextBoom fatal markers: 0
UnsatisfiedLink / dlopen markers: 0
All three cases reached com.smartisanos.textboom/.BoomActivity.
```

Preview-image regression:

```text
/sdcard/.boom/imageboom.jpg
sha256=85c8d407caa2df8a0f644182f3bc2fb9c0caebcaaecb4c531de344bcde645f6a
mtime=2026-06-20 23:10:41 +0800
size=154521
dims=1080x773
owner=root:everybody
mode=0660
```

The OCR chips match the active screen in each case, but the result-page preview
keeps showing the old Runtime/WebView/WebGPU image because
`BoomChipPage.loadOcrImageView()` loads the fixed `FileUtils.OCR_IMAGE_PATH`.
The fixed file did not change before/after any of the three live BOOM_IMAGE
runs. The repeated `Permission denied` stack in the captured window is from
`com.android.settings.HandleEventService`, not directly from TextBoom
`FileUtils`, so it is recorded as log noise until a narrower trace proves
otherwise.

Current verdict:

```text
v0.41.1 passes the broader PP-OCR model and live TextBoom runtime gate for
basic quality, latency, memory, stability, and logcat fatal/native-link checks.
It does not yet pass the result-page preview-image correctness gate.
```

Next patch:

```text
Move TextBoom preview storage away from the single public
/sdcard/.boom/imageboom.jpg path. Use a TextBoom-owned app-specific per-run file
or cache-busted URI, then update BoomChipPage image loading and size lookup.
Do not delete legacy CsOcr/Intsig code or ocr_key until this preview regression
is fixed and live-verified.
```

Verdict:

```text
v0.42 is live-stable for boot, package state, and PP-OCR inference, but it does
not fix the result-page preview path. Android/data is not a safe direct-file
target for this legacy TextBoom path on R2 Android 11. The next candidate should
move the fixed preview path to Android/media or use a Context-backed URI/per-run
file design.
```

BOOM_IMAGE regression:

```text
command:

  OCR_IMAGE_PATH=/sdcard/Android/media/com.smartisanos.textboom/.boom/imageboom.jpg \

result:

cases:
  smartisax_home: count_text=共 226 字, token_count=29, fatal=0, unsatisfied=0
  settings_main: count_text=共 33 字, token_count=23, fatal=0, unsatisfied=0
  textboom_app_details: count_text=共 40 字, token_count=22, fatal=0, unsatisfied=0
preview file:
  /sdcard/Android/media/com.smartisanos.textboom/.boom/imageboom.jpg missing in all cases
log evidence:

  /storage/emulated/0/Android/media/com.smartisanos.textboom/.boom/imageboom.jpg
report:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-live-ocr-regression/20260621-v0421-preview-media-path-live/
```

Root cause:

```text
v0.42.1 proved Android/media is read by BoomChipPage, but it still did not
write imageboom.jpg. Static smali plus live log timing show the live launch path
is:

  startOcrCropped(1)
    -> dealSaveBitmapResult(bitmap)
    -> mOcrApi.startOcr(... LocalPpOcrApi ...)
    -> startBoomActivity(...)

That path does not enter the old TaskHandler branch containing:

  FileUtils.saveBMtoLocal(bitmap, "imageboom.jpg")

So v0.42.2 must save the preview bitmap in dealSaveBitmapResult before starting
LocalPpOcrApi.
```

Space intervention during v0.42.2 build:

```text
symptom:
  v0.42.2 first super build failed with No space left on device
df before:
  /System/Volumes/Data available about 977Mi
cleanup:

    super-otatrust-v0.39-sidebar-font-ocr-deleted.sparse.img
    super-otatrust-v0.40-textboom-ppocr-noop-adapter.sparse.img
    super-otatrust-v0.41-textboom-ppocr-runtime-adapter.sparse.img
    super-otatrust-v0.42-textboom-ppocr-preview-path.sparse.img
    system-otatrust-v0.42-textboom-ppocr-preview-path.img
    super-otatrust-v0.42.1-textboom-ppocr-preview-media-path.sparse.img
    system-otatrust-v0.42.1-textboom-ppocr-preview-media-path.img
retained:
  v0.4 rollback sparse
  v0.41.1 live-proven baseline sparse/system image
  v0.42.2 preview-save candidate sparse/system image
  v0.35.2 retained product_b image
df after final cleanup:
  /System/Volumes/Data available about 24Gi
```

## 2026-06-21 - v0.42.2 TextBoom preview save-before-OCR offline candidate

Purpose:

```text
Keep the Android/media preview path from v0.42.1, but patch
BoomOcrActivity.dealSaveBitmapResult(bitmap) to call:

  FileUtils.saveBMtoLocal(bitmap, "imageboom.jpg")

before:

  IOcrApi.startOcr(...)

This preserves PP-OCR's in-memory bitmap input while giving BoomActivity's
legacy preview loader a fresh imageboom.jpg.
```

```text
TextBoom CsOcr and TextBoom-local Intsig/CamScanner code from classes2.dex.

Retained:
  original AndroidManifest.xml
  AndroidManifest.xml meta-data android:name="ocr_key"
  LocalPpOcrApi / LocalPpOcrRuntime
  PP-OCRv6 small det/rec ONNX models
  Android/media preview-save path
  arm32/arm64 ONNX Runtime and OpenCV libraries

Removed:
  com.smartisanos.textboom.ocr.CsOcr smali
  com.smartisanos.textboom.ocr.CsOcr lambda smali
  TextBoom-local com.intsig.csopen smali package

Deferred:
  CamScanner wording in resources.arsc
  any future ocr_key manifest removal until a manifest/signature carrier plan
```

Boundary:

```text
v0.43b is the current live-verified TextBoom/OCR line. It removes the legacy
CsOcr/Intsig executable path without depending on CamScanner. It deliberately
keeps manifest ocr_key because v0.43a proved manifest edits can make
PackageManager reject this stock-shell system APK.

Residual product decision: v0.43b now resolves as primaryCpuAbi=arm64-v8a.
The state is live-proven, but the three BOOM_IMAGE cases report total PSS
around 359-386 MB. Decide whether to accept this arm64 state or build v0.43c to
force the earlier armeabi-v7a ABI before doing the separate resources.arsc
CamScanner wording cleanup.
```

```text
  BOOM_IMAGE attempts.
```

Conclusion:

```text
v0.43c boots and preserves system basics, BOOM_TEXT, WebView, Smartisax, and
Sidebar, but fails the intended ABI and BOOM_IMAGE gates.

Deleting APK-internal lib/arm64-v8a entries and /system/app/TextBoom/lib/arm64
does not force PackageManager to recalculate TextBoom to armeabi-v7a. The app
still launches under app_process64, and without arm64 OpenCV/ORT libraries the
PP-OCR runtime cannot initialize.

Accepted TextBoom/OCR base remains v0.43b. Next action should be either a
user-confirmed rollback to v0.43b, or a v0.43d design that changes the
PackageManager scan/codePath boundary without editing AndroidManifest.xml or
ocr_key.
```

## 2026-06-21 - v0.43d TextBoom codePath ABI offline candidate PASS

Goal:

```text
Build a v0.43d offline candidate that does not edit AndroidManifest.xml or
ocr_key, but changes the PackageManager scan/codePath boundary so TextBoom is
rescanned from a fresh system app directory. The hypothesis is that a new
codePath plus absent arm64 native libraries will make PackageManager recalculate
primaryCpuAbi=armeabi-v7a, unlike v0.43c where the same /system/app/TextBoom
path stayed cached as arm64-v8a.
```

Deliberate lockscreen decision:

```text
Do not bake a Keyguard bypass into this ROM candidate. That would mix an
unrelated SystemUI/Keyguard/SettingsProvider risk into the TextBoom ABI test.

Instead, tools/r2-verify-v0.42-textboom-preview-path.sh now supports
AUTO_DISMISS_KEYGUARD=1 for live verification. It only performs UI input
attempts on the current no-password test device, then still verifies the window
state. It does not flash, reboot, mutate packages, clear /data, or modify the
ROM Keyguard implementation.
```

CodePath design:

```text
old public package path:
  /system/app/TextBoom/TextBoom.apk
new public package path:
  /system/app/TextBoomArm32/TextBoomArm32.apk
old public path state:
  absent
old stock APK retained as:
  /system/app/TextBoom/.TextBoom.apk.smartisax-v0.43d-textboom-codepath-arm32-abi-old-codepath-held
  non-.apk suffix so PackageManager should not scan it
target arm32 native library path:
  /system/app/TextBoomArm32/lib/arm
target arm64 native library path:
  /system/app/TextBoomArm32/lib/arm64 absent
```

Build command:

```bash
VARIANT=v0.43d-textboom-codepath-arm32-abi \
PURPOSE='Move TextBoom to a fresh system app codePath /system/app/TextBoomArm32 and hide the old public TextBoom.apk so PackageManager must rescan the same package from a new directory while retaining v0.43b CsOcr/Intsig deletion and v0.42.2 preview-save behavior.' \
RESULT_NAME=PASS_BUILD_V043D_TEXTBOOM_CODEPATH_ARM32_ABI \
TEXTBOOM_PREVIEW_APK="$PWD/hard-rom/build/apk/TextBoom-ppocr-csocr-intsig-delete-force-arm32.apk" \
TEXTBOOM_PREVIEW_APK_SHA256=0627630d5f6e06a41b9f21c7a5cacc82be571eec4984d90ef715f681be6644d7 \
NEW_OCR_DIR=/Android/media/com.smartisanos.textboom/.boom \
NEW_OCR_PATH=/sdcard/Android/media/com.smartisanos.textboom/.boom/imageboom.jpg \
EXPECT_LEGACY_CSOCR_REMOVED=1 \
EXPECT_OCR_KEY_REMOVED=0 \
REMOVE_TEXTBOOM_ARM64_LIBS=1 \
TEXTBOOM_ARM64_LIBS_EXPECTED=0 \
TEXTBOOM_CHANGED_PAYLOADS='classes2.dex_from_v043b_plus_codepath_move' \
TEXTBOOM_CODEPATH_MOVE=1 \
TEXTBOOM_TARGET_DIR=/system/app/TextBoomArm32 \
TEXTBOOM_TARGET_APK_NAME=TextBoomArm32.apk \
tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.42-textboom-preview-path.sh
```

Build result:

```text

  PASS_BUILD_V043D_TEXTBOOM_CODEPATH_ARM32_ABI
```

BOOM_IMAGE regression:

```text
command:
  RUN_ID=20260621-v043d-codepath-arm32-abi-live \
  OCR_IMAGE_PATH=/sdcard/Android/media/com.smartisanos.textboom/.boom/imageboom.jpg \
  tools/r2-textboom-live-ocr-regression.sh
report:
  hard-rom/inspect/textboom-live-ocr-regression/
    20260621-v043d-codepath-arm32-abi-live/
result:
  CAPTURED_TEXTBOOM_LIVE_OCR_REGRESSION
cases:
  smartisax_home        launched BoomOcrActivity, image changed, source-screen tokens, PSS=38680 KB
  settings_main         launched BoomOcrActivity, image changed, source-screen tokens, PSS=38112 KB
  textboom_app_details  launched BoomOcrActivity, image changed, source-screen tokens, PSS=38225 KB
aggregate:
  fatal_marker_count=0
  unsatisfied_link_marker_count=1 per case
  permission_denied_marker_count=2 per case
logcat:
  OpenCVUtils: Failed to initialize OpenCV: dlopen failed:
    library "libopencv_java4.so" not found
  TextBoomLocalPpOcr: local PP-OCR failed
  java.lang.IllegalStateException: OpenCV init failed
```

Conclusion:

```text
v0.43d boots and preserves basic system state, WebView, Smartisax, Sidebar, and
BOOM_TEXT. It partially succeeds by moving the live TextBoom PackageManager
codePath to /system/app/TextBoomArm32.

It fails the intended ABI and BOOM_IMAGE gates. PackageManager still records
primaryCpuAbi=arm64-v8a, TextBoom runs under app_process64, and the arm64
dalvik-cache path is regenerated for the new codePath. Because arm64 native
libraries are absent by design, PP-OCR cannot initialize OpenCV.

Changing only the system app codePath is therefore insufficient to force ABI
recalculation to armeabi-v7a. Accepted TextBoom/OCR base remains v0.43b unless
a v0.43e plan succeeds. The current live phone is on v0.43d rejected state; a
rollback to v0.43b requires a separate explicit confirmation.
```

## 2026-06-22 - v0.43e TextBoom codePath arm64 runtime repair offline candidate PASS

User direction:

```text
不必先回滚 真机现在就是测试机 只要不影响业务推进暂时有点问题的ROM都不用回退 直接往后继续推进修复设计即可
```

Design:

```text
variant:
  v0.43e-textboom-codepath-arm64-runtime-repair
source behavior:
  v0.43d successfully moved TextBoom's PackageManager codePath to
  /system/app/TextBoomArm32 but live PackageManager still selected arm64-v8a.
repair:
  accept arm64-v8a for the current forward path
  keep the /system/app/TextBoomArm32 codePath boundary
  keep APK-internal lib/arm64-v8a/* absent
  restore target system arm64 ORT/OpenCV runtime libraries under
    /system/app/TextBoomArm32/lib/arm64
expected live fix:
  BOOM_IMAGE should no longer fail at OpenCV dlopen for libopencv_java4.so
```

Builder/script changes:

```text

  tools/r2-verify-v0.42-textboom-preview-path.sh
  tools/r2-live-flash-preflight.sh
important verifier split:
  TEXTBOOM_APK_ARM64_LIBS_EXPECTED controls APK-internal lib/arm64-v8a/*
  TEXTBOOM_ARM64_LIBS_EXPECTED controls system app target lib/arm64
```

```text
B-slot boot all pass.

Next work should continue from v0.43e into CamScanner resource-string cleanup
and broader PP-OCR quality/memory regression. True armeabi-v7a forcing remains
a separate PackageManager policy investigation.
```

## 2026-06-22 - v0.44 TextBoom legacy OCR cleanup APK-only candidate

Purpose:

```text
Continue from the live-proven v0.43e TextBoom/OCR base and remove remaining
CamScanner/Intsig behavior and wording without touching AndroidManifest.xml.

Keep manifest ocr_key retained for this gate because v0.43a proved changing
TextBoom's original-cert stock-shell AndroidManifest.xml makes PackageManager
ignore the package after boot.
```

Static finding:

```text
v0.43e already removed TextBoom-local CsOcr and com.intsig.csopen smali, but
two legacy surfaces remained:

1. resources.arsc still had CamScanner/扫描全能王 error strings:
   ocr_camscanner_no_permission
   ocr_camscanner_permission_denied
   ocr_recognize_not_available
   ocr_recognize_uninstall

2. BoomAccessOcrActivity still had a connectivity branch:
   if network connected -> BoomAccessOcrActivity$OcrThread
   OcrThread -> getDataFromOnLine(...)
   URL -> http://imgs-sandbox.intsig.net/icr/recognize_document?...password=...

This meant the accessibility OCR path could still use the old online OCR path
when the device had network connectivity, even though BOOM_IMAGE already used
LocalPpOcrApi.
```

APK-only build:

```text
command:
  tools/r2-build-textboom-ppocr-legacy-ocr-cleanup-apk.sh
source APK:
  hard-rom/build/apk/TextBoom-ppocr-csocr-intsig-delete-force-arm32.apk
source sha256:
  0627630d5f6e06a41b9f21c7a5cacc82be571eec4984d90ef715f681be6644d7
output APK:
  hard-rom/build/apk/TextBoom-ppocr-legacy-ocr-cleanup.apk
output sha256:
  fe761609aac2be4eade7bc747bfdc429497f5e43627a4f19b4d76b5ce22faa26
changed zip entries:
  classes2.dex
  resources.arsc
removed zip entries:
  none
manifest:
  original AndroidManifest.xml retained
  ocr_key retained
```

Changes:

```text
classes2.dex:
  BoomAccessOcrActivity.doOcr no longer calls
  BoomAccessOcrActivity->isConnected()Z to choose online OCR.

  The branch is forced to the existing LocalPpOcrApi path, so accessibility OCR
  now follows local PP-OCR regardless of network state.

  The hardcoded Intsig/CamScanner URL is removed from BoomAccessOcrActivity.

resources.arsc:
  CamScanner/扫描全能王 wording is removed.

  ocr_camscanner_no_permission -> ocr_storage_no_permission
  ocr_camscanner_permission_denied -> ocr_storage_permission_denied

  The same resource IDs are preserved; only resource names/values change.
```

Offline verification:

```text
zip boundary:
  changed_entries=classes2.dex,resources.arsc
  removed_entries=
  added_entries=

resources.arsc layout:
  method=0 (STORED)
  data_offset=2518152
  data_offset_mod4=0
  size=590532

semantic audit:
  classes2_legacy_url_absent=true
  classes2_connectivity_gate_removed=true
  classes2_local_ppocr_retained=true
  resources_camscanner_wording_absent=true
  resources_storage_symbols_present=true
  manifest_ocr_key_retained=true

independent strings scan:
  classes2 contains LocalPpOcrApi and LocalPpOcrRuntime
  classes2 has no imgs-sandbox/Intsig/CsOcr/CSOCR/CamScanner/ocr_camscanner hits
  resources.arsc has storage-neutral OCR strings and no CamScanner/扫描全能王 hits
```

Conclusion:

```text
v0.44 is currently APK-only and is not yet a ROM image, live preflight, or flash
target. The next step is to promote this APK into a v0.44 system_b/super image
based on live-proven v0.43e once disk space is sufficient, then run live
preflight and request explicit flash confirmation.
```

## 2026-06-22 - PackageManager policy map and v0.pm0 services.jar no-op gate

Reason:

```text
Several recent failures were PackageManager state decisions rather than simple
app logic failures:

  v0.26a.1 reused stale /data/system/package_cache ParsedPackage data.
  v0.36 hit Android 11 resources.arsc STORED/alignment parsing.
  v0.37a was shadowed by an updated-system /data/app TextBoom.
  v0.43a made TextBoom disappear after AndroidManifest.xml/ocr_key removal.
  v0.43c/v0.43d kept primaryCpuAbi=arm64-v8a despite APK/lib/codePath changes.

The next deep-ROM route should therefore investigate PackageManager itself, but
only through explicit Smartisax allowlists rather than global bypasses.
```

Static source findings:

```text
PackageCacher:
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__framework__services.jar/sources/com/android/server/pm/parsing/PackageCacher.java

  cache key is packageFile.getName() + '-' + flags.
  cache is accepted when packageFile mtime is older than cache file mtime.
  This matches earlier cachebump behavior.

PackageManagerService.scanPackageOnlyLI:
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__framework__services.jar/sources/com/android/server/pm/PackageManagerService.java

  if PackageSetting already has primaryCpuAbiString and scan flags do not force
  ABI derivation, PMS reuses stored ABI.
  This matches v0.43c/v0.43d primaryCpuAbi=arm64-v8a behavior.

PackageAbiHelperImpl:
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/jadx/system__system__framework__services.jar/sources/com/android/server/pm/PackageAbiHelperImpl.java

  bundled app ABI derives from system lib/lib64 paths, package-local lib/<isa>,
  and oat directories.

Signature reconciliation:
  PackageManagerService.reconcilePackagesLocked still calls
  PackageManagerServiceUtils.verifySignatures().
  The policy route must not globally bypass signatures or sharedUserId checks.
```

Design note:

```text
docs/research/package-manager-policy-map.md
```

Offline services.jar no-op builder:

```text
script:
  tools/r2-build-services-pm-noop-jar.sh

input:
  reverse/smartisan-8.5.3-rom-static/raw/system/system/framework/services.jar

input sha256:
  45945c1d1f9f25be8b1db31df5d417504634305d4033e9421f7d3cd416057da6

process:
  apktool decode stock services.jar
  apktool rebuild without smali edits
  merge only rebuilt classes.dex/classes2.dex into the stock jar shell
  zipalign output
  verify ZIP/DEX structure
  decode final output and copy key PMS smali evidence

output:
  hard-rom/build/framework/services-pm-noop-roundtrip.jar

output sha256:
  30ff020c9dead1afba480dfc075b50454723296376feae0b20a1a58e82f763bc
```

Verification:

```text
report:
  hard-rom/inspect/v0.pm0-services-jar-noop/services-pm-noop-roundtrip-report.txt

result:
  services_pm_noop_jar=ok

changed entries:
  classes.dex
  classes2.dex

stock shell:
  non-dex entries preserved
  META-INF/MANIFEST.MF retained
  all entries STORED

smali evidence:
  hard-rom/inspect/v0.pm0-services-jar-noop/smali-evidence/PackageManagerService.smali
  hard-rom/inspect/v0.pm0-services-jar-noop/smali-evidence/PackageAbiHelperImpl.smali
  hard-rom/inspect/v0.pm0-services-jar-noop/smali-evidence/PackageCacher.smali
```

Conclusion:

```text
v0.pm0-services-jar-noop is currently an offline JAR-only PASS.
It is not yet a system_b image, super image, live preflight, or flash target.
The next gate is to place this services.jar into the current system_b baseline,
verify ext4/FEC and dumped jar hash offline, then run live preflight and request
explicit flash confirmation before testing whether system_server boots.
```

## 2026-06-22 - v0.pm0 services.jar no-op system_b image gate

Reason:

```text
Promote the PackageManager services.jar no-op from a JAR-only artifact into the
smallest ROM image gate before any real PMS policy edits. This tests whether a
roundtripped services.jar can sit in system_b with stale services preopt removed
and a valid FEC hashtree footer.
```

Inputs:

```text

verifier:
  tools/r2-verify-v0.pm0-services-jar-noop.sh --offline-image
```

Important image-space finding:

```text
The v0.43e system_b ext4 image had no usable free blocks for a second
services.jar copy. The first held-stock attempt failed while writing the
candidate jar.

The accepted build therefore uses a target-specific narrow exception:
  1. audit public arm64 services.art/services.odex/services.vdex block owners
  2. remove those public stale preopt files with debugfs rm
  3. audit public stock services.jar block owners
  4. remove public stock services.jar with debugfs rm
  5. write the no-op services.jar at /system/framework/services.jar

Audits:
  services.art:  inode=3105 blocks=571 bad_owner_count=0
  services.odex: inode=3106 blocks=8047 bad_owner_count=0
  services.vdex: inode=3107 blocks=36 bad_owner_count=0
  services.jar:  inode=3132 blocks=4051 bad_owner_count=0

Free blocks:
  before: 407
  after preopt remove: 9061
  after final jar replacement: 9056
```

## 2026-06-22 - PackageManager pm1 cache policy design selected

Reason:

```text
Enter the first real PackageManager behavior policy after v0.pm0 proved the
services.jar no-op framework gate on the live B slot.
```

Design document:

```text
docs/research/package-manager-pm1-cache-policy-design.md
```

Selected policy:

```text
pm1:
  allowlisted package parser cache read-bypass for Smartisax-managed boot-scan
  package paths.

initial allowlist:
  /system/app/SmartisaxShell
  /system/app/TextBoomArm32
  /system/app/TextBoom
  /system/priv-app/Sidebar
```

Source finding:

```text
PackageCacher:
  cache key is packageFile.getName() + '-' + flags
  cache is accepted when package mtime is older than cache mtime

PackageParser2:
  useCaches controls only whether an existing cache is read
  after a real parse, the parser still writes a fresh cache entry when mCacher
  exists

ParallelPackageParser:
  boot-time parallel directory scans currently pass useCaches=true for every
  scan file

PackageManagerService single-package scan:
  already builds PackageParser2 with cacheDir=null and calls parsePackage with
  useCaches=false
```

Patch shape:

```text
new helper:
  com.android.server.pm.SmartisaxPackagePolicy

pure helper:
  static boolean shouldBypassPackageCache(File scanFile)

edited call site:
  com.android.server.pm.ParallelPackageParser.parsePackage(File, int)

behavior:
  useCaches = !SmartisaxPackagePolicy.shouldBypassPackageCache(scanFile)
```

Guardrails:

```text
Do not modify PackageCacher, PackageParser2, PackageManagerService,
PackageAbiHelperImpl, Settings, or PackageManagerServiceUtils in pm1.

Do not globally disable package cache with pm.boot.disable_package_cache.
Do not clear /data/system/package_cache as part of pm1.
Do not change signature verification, sharedUserId checks, /data/app
precedence, updated-system shadow behavior, or primaryCpuAbi reuse.
```

Conclusion:

```text
The next implementation candidate is v0.pm1-pms-cache-allowlist on top of
v0.pm0-services-jar-noop. It should change only services.jar dex entries, add
SmartisaxPackagePolicy, patch ParallelPackageParser, rebuild system_b/sparse
with the existing v0.pm0 services.jar image pipeline, and require explicit
flash confirmation before live testing.
```

## 2026-06-22 - USB mass_storage source audit after HandShaker deletion

Reason:

```text
The user observed that the Mac still shows a Smartisan transfer-tool mounted
disk even though the ROM-level HandShaker APK has been deleted. Audit the USB
mass-storage source separately before changing anything.
```

Conclusion:

```text
The Mac-visible "Smartisan transfer tool" disk is vendor USB gadget
mass_storage backed by /vendor/etc/cdrom_install.iso. It is not produced by the
Android HandShaker APK package. Removing that mounted disk should be a separate
vendor-image candidate that keeps MTP and ADB intact.
```

```text
override, and pm1 scripts pass the pm0 lpdump extent explicitly.
```

## 2026-06-22 - v0.usb1 Smartisan virtual CD-ROM removal offline + preflight PASS

Intent:

```text
Build the first dedicated vendor_b candidate for removing the Mac-visible
"Smartisan transfer tool" virtual disk after HandShaker APK removal.

Boundary:
  source baseline: v0.kg1-smartisax-skip-keyguard
  patched partition: vendor_b only
  no system/services.jar changes
  no ISO deletion in this first cut
  no live flash yet
```

Design:

```text
The audit proved the disk comes from vendor USB gadget mass_storage backed by:
  /vendor/etc/cdrom_install.iso

The first candidate keeps the ISO as an inert retained file and instead removes
all mass_storage.0 symlinks from vendor USB configfs compositions. This should
stop macOS from mounting the Smartisan transfer-tool ISO while keeping ADB and
MTP routes present.

Because live /data already stores:
  persist.sys.usb.config=mtp,diag,diag_mdm,mass_storage,adb

the old persisted config trigger is deliberately retained, but its body no
longer attaches mass_storage.0 to /config/usb_gadget/g1/configs/b.1/f*.
```

Important implementation lesson:

```text
Do not use the usual hard-link held-stock-inode pattern for these vendor init
files on this image. A trial build showed debugfs ln can create inode-0 bad
directory entries for /build.prop and /etc/init/hw/*.rc paths while e2fsck -fn
still returns clean.

Final route:
  1. erase vendor_b AVB footer
  2. fsck the pure ext4 image
  3. audit each target file's physical blocks with debugfs blocks/icheck
  4. only after unique-block proof, use direct debugfs rm + write
  5. restore mode/uid/gid/SELinux label
  6. e2fsck -fy, e2fsck -fn
  7. rebuild vendor_b AVB/FEC roots=2
```

Tools added:

```text
tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom.sh
tools/r2-verify-v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom.sh
tools/r2-live-flash-preflight.sh v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom
```

Build:

```text
tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom.sh

report:
  hard-rom/inspect/v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom/build-v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom-20260622-155655.txt

  sha256=450c5e1e34b20a7fd66422c96e359bf949e3968a62c3f6f73db81a229706518c

source vendor_b:
  sha256=d6e09ff25c612cc7f01c05f455646926019e17b1fe73b98f6ab7c0d5b69489f6
  extent=vendor_b=15439872:1696608

candidate vendor_b:
  hard-rom/build/vendor-otatrust-v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom.img
  sha256=92cc0620019295f7e2ceeb982c011441ba81d65a46376c07eab032827d668afd

candidate sparse:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom.sparse.img
  sha256=1608da03f036a4e9d4972d7c892fd018903e603a299040e5464a1512547829bc

result:
  PASS_BUILD_V0USB1_NO_SMARTISAN_CDROM
```

Modified vendor files:

```text
/etc/init/hw/init.qcom.usb.rc
  sha256=4a728f6b42177f8db507c9ef58fede21faa836a0a4f8bcc2352c1ef9898b5917
  removes 4 configfs symlink lines that attached mass_storage.0
  keeps ADB and MTP symlink routes
  keeps old persisted mtp,diag,diag_mdm,mass_storage,adb trigger

/etc/init/hw/init.qcom.rc
  sha256=0509ec5fb6c9e5dd267348c844d59f867aa6312a48f7a1456160e76953c378bc
  changes charger fallback from persist.sys.usb.config mass_storage to charging
```

Retained vendor files:

```text
/build.prop
  persist.service.cdrom.enable remains 1 in the ROM file. This is intentional
  for v0.usb1 because the live property may already be /data-persisted anyway,
  and the effective removal gate is the absence of active mass_storage.0
  config symlinks.

/etc/cdrom_install.iso
  sha256=f4a5f3f482c9b091557a9b4366c8b808fa1cfd4d8c5f7afdbc11af12b0af25a0
  retained as inert payload for this first candidate.
```

Offline verifier:


## 2026-06-22 - v0.usb2 physical CD-ROM ISO removal offline + preflight PASS

Scope:

```text
Build a vendor_b-only physical cleanup follow-up on top of live-proven
v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom. The candidate removes
/vendor/etc/cdrom_install.iso, preserves the v0.usb1 USB init behavior, and
zeroes only old ISO blocks that remain free after deletion.
```

Build script:

```text
tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.usb2-physical-cdrom-iso-delete.sh
```

Source:

```text
source sparse:
  hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.usb1-no-smartisan-cdrom.sparse.img
  sha256=1608da03f036a4e9d4972d7c892fd018903e603a299040e5464a1512547829bc
source vendor_b:
  sha256=92cc0620019295f7e2ceeb982c011441ba81d65a46376c07eab032827d668afd
```

Block audit:

```text
ISO inode=536
ISO size=41353216
logical block entries=9462
unique physical blocks=9392
internal duplicate block entries=70
block owner audit:
  hard-rom/work/v0.usb2-physical-cdrom-iso-delete/cdrom-iso-block-owner-audit.json
```

Deletion and zeroing result:

```text
debugfs rm /etc/cdrom_install.iso: PASS
e2fsck after delete: PASS
old ISO blocks free after delete: 9391
old ISO blocks reassigned after delete: 1
zeroed old free blocks: 9391
preserved reassigned block:
  block 28776 -> inode 2994 -> /media/icon/cn.kuwo.player/logo
payload string scan:
  HandShaker.dmg absent
  Guide_to_Transferring_Files_Between_Your_Phone_and_a_Mac.pdf absent
  HandShaker_Win8&Later_Web_Setup.exe absent
```

Important lesson:

```text
debugfs icheck before deletion reported the shared/deduped block 28776 as owned
by the ISO inode. After deletion and e2fsck, the same block was reassigned to
/media/icon/cn.kuwo.player/logo. For vendor shared_blocks cleanup, do not zero
all old target blocks purely from pre-delete icheck. Delete first, fsck, classify
old blocks again, zero only free blocks, and preserve reassigned blocks.
```

### 2026-06-22 - v0.wadb1 Smartisax priv-app + wireless ADB entry candidate

Goal:

```text
Promote Smartisax from /system/app to /system/priv-app and add a privapp
permission whitelist so Smartisax can expose a guarded wireless ADB control
entry. The WebView Javascript bridge is attached only on the local
file:///android_asset/shell/ surface and removed before external page loads.
```

### 2026-06-22: v0.wadb2 Smartisax Wireless ADB Current-Wi-Fi Repair

Goal:

```text
Fix the v0.wadb1 wireless ADB Enable path. v0.wadb1 promoted Smartisax to a
priv-app and granted MANAGE_DEBUGGING/WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS, but the app-side
BSSID lookup was still permission/namespace fragile and fell back to APatch
root, where /system/bin/kp was not visible from the app process.

v0.wadb2 keeps the Smartisax privileged entry and changes the contract:
Smartisax sends a private current-Wi-Fi sentinel, and services.jar resolves the
real current Wi-Fi BSSID inside system_server before calling
AdbKeyStore.addTrustedNetwork().
```

Inputs:

```text
source_variant=v0.wadb1-smartisax-priv-wireless-adb
source_sparse=hard-rom/build/super-otatrust-v0.wadb1-smartisax-priv-wireless-adb.sparse.img
source_sparse_sha256=12e0a42afe1a39fa63948568a7bce84804052019584eaacb46b37151c6ae18cc
source_system_b_sha256=013d335d8ed3bda9420f553b62eb5ca7de0c23cb21b9d3a35da664e8dfee0f8b

SmartisaxShell.apk sha256:
  62386bd8a33ea086259621190a4018aa2407556066adfe9da7526685c813ed1a

services-wadb2-current-wifi-adb.jar sha256:
  59e1174901e914684a15e1bb22f82bc5683e23c91ec9a74123f7367826ab8ce2
```

Image build:

```text
command:
  tools/r2-hardrom-build-v0.wadb2-smartisax-wireless-adb-current-wifi.sh

report:
```

```text
command:
```

```text
  android.debug.IAdbManager$Stub.asInterface [interface android.os.IBinder]
  root[125]

Conclusion:
  The v0.wadb2 services.jar current-Wi-Fi policy is installed, but the
  Smartisax APK does not reach it because the app-side hidden API reflection
  uses getMethod() on IAdbManager$Stub.asInterface(). A repair candidate should
  use getDeclaredMethod()/setAccessible(true). PackageManager also still shows
  Smartisax versionCode=2 because the PM1 bypass policy only covered
  /system/app/SmartisaxShell, not the new /system/priv-app/SmartisaxShell path.
```

### 2026-06-22: v0.wadb2.2 Smartisax Wireless ADB Binder Transact Repair

Goal:

```text
Repair Smartisax wireless ADB after v0.wadb2.1 by replacing hidden
IAdbManager reflection with raw Binder transact calls:
  transaction 4  allowWirelessDebugging(alwaysAllow, bssid)
  transaction 5  denyWirelessDebugging()
  transaction 10 getAdbWirelessPort()

Keep v0.wadb2.1 services.jar unchanged, including the current-Wi-Fi sentinel
policy and /system/priv-app/SmartisaxShell PackageManager cache bypass.
```

### 2026-06-22: v0.mirror0 scrcpy HandShaker Replacement Live Proof

Goal:

```text
Build the first non-ROM HandShaker replacement proof for Mac-side R2 screen
mirroring/control, using the current USB ADB and v0.wadb2.2 wireless ADB
transports.
```

Mac-side tool install:

```text
command:
  brew install scrcpy

result:
  installed scrcpy 4.0 and libusb 1.0.30 under /opt/homebrew

version:
  scrcpy 4.0
  SDL 3.4.8 / 3.4.10
  libavcodec 62.28.101 / 62.28.102
  libusb 1.0.30
```

ADB transports:

```text
command:
  adb devices -l

result:
  bb12d264               device usb:2-1.2 product:darwin device:darwin
  192.168.31.103:42701   device product:darwin device:darwin
```

USB video smoke:

```text
command:
  scrcpy -s bb12d264 --no-window --no-audio --time-limit=8 \
    --record=hard-rom/inspect/v0.mirror0-scrcpy-live-proof/scrcpy-usb-smoke.mp4

result:
  scrcpy server pushed
  device identified as SMARTISAN unknown / Android 11
  recording completed

ffprobe:
  codec_name=h264
  width=1080
  height=2340
  avg_frame_rate=10/1
  duration=1.100000
```

USB video plus ADB control smoke:

```text
commands:
  scrcpy -s bb12d264 --no-window --no-audio --time-limit=12 \
    --record=hard-rom/inspect/v0.mirror0-scrcpy-live-proof/scrcpy-usb-control-smoke.mp4
  adb -s bb12d264 shell input keyevent 3
  adb -s bb12d264 shell input swipe 900 1800 900 900 300

result:
  ADB input reached the live phone while the scrcpy recording session was active
  recording completed

ffprobe:
  codec_name=h264
  width=1080
  height=2340
  avg_frame_rate=3960000/1083157
  duration=12.035078
```

Wireless ADB video smoke:

```text
command:
  scrcpy -s 192.168.31.103:42701 --no-window --no-audio --time-limit=8 \
    --record=hard-rom/inspect/v0.mirror0-scrcpy-live-proof/scrcpy-wireless-smoke.mp4

result:
  scrcpy server pushed through tcpip ADB
  recording completed

ffprobe:
  codec_name=h264
  width=1080
  height=2340
  avg_frame_rate=5280/1757
  duration=7.320833
```

Mac wrapper:

```text
added:
  tools/r2-mirror.sh

usage:
  tools/r2-mirror.sh [auto|usb|wireless] [scrcpy-args...]
  tools/r2-mirror.sh record <output.mp4> [seconds] [auto|usb|wireless]
  tools/r2-mirror.sh connect-wireless [host:port]
  tools/r2-mirror.sh portal-url [auto|usb|wireless] [port]
  tools/r2-mirror.sh devices

self-test command:
  tools/r2-mirror.sh record \
    hard-rom/inspect/v0.mirror0-scrcpy-live-proof/r2-mirror-script-usb-smoke.mp4 \
    5 usb

result:
  script selected bb12d264
  scrcpy recording completed

wireless reconnect self-test:
  tools/r2-mirror.sh connect-wireless
  Wireless adb target online: 192.168.31.103:42701

LAN portal URL self-test:
  tools/r2-mirror.sh portal-url
  http://192.168.31.103:37601
```

Interactive window smoke:

```text
command:
  tools/r2-mirror.sh usb --no-audio --time-limit=8

result:
  Starting R2 mirror via bb12d264
  Renderer: metal
  Texture: 1080x2340
  Time limit reached
```

Conclusion:

```text
v0.mirror0-scrcpy-live-proof passes. USB and wireless scrcpy are viable as the
first HandShaker replacement layer for screen viewing/control. The next step is
v0.mirror1-mac-wrapper polish. The browser-access Smartisax Device Portal and
TNT/Desktop-mode reuse remain separate follow-up lines.
```

### 2026-06-22: v0.portal0 Smartisax Device Portal Design

Design document:

```text
docs/research/smartisax-device-portal-design.md
```

Scope:

```text
Use scrcpy as the proven screen/control layer.
Add a browser-accessible Smartisax portal as a separate local management plane.
Expose it directly over LAN through the R2 Wi-Fi IP in the first product route.
```

Recommended architecture:

```text
Mac browser:
  http://<r2_wifi_ip>:37601

R2:
  Smartisax DevicePortalService listens on the current wlan0 IPv4 address
  Smartisax UI explicitly starts/stops the service
  all /api/* endpoints require a session token
  pairing code is displayed on the phone
```

First endpoint set:

```text
GET /api/status
GET /api/files?path=/sdcard
GET /api/file?path=/sdcard/...
POST /api/wadb/enable
POST /api/wadb/disable
POST /api/files/upload
```

Deferred:

```text
WebRTC/MediaProjection screen streaming
root filesystem browser
TNT/Desktop-mode reuse
```

Next candidate:

```text
v0.portal1-smartisax-lan-portal-noop
  add DevicePortalService
  serve token-gated /api/status only
  extend tools/r2-mirror.sh with portal-url/portal-open
```

Mac wrapper update:

```text
tools/r2-mirror.sh portal-url
  output: http://192.168.31.103:37601
```

### 2026-06-22: v0.portal1 Smartisax LAN Portal Noop Build And Preflight

Variant:


### 2026-06-22: v0.portal2 Smartisax Remote Screen Control Build And Preflight PASS

Goal:

```text
Add the first browser-visible R2 screen stream and pointer control path to the
Smartisax LAN Device Portal, while keeping the already live-proven v0.portal1
pairing, Wi-Fi bind, services.jar, wireless ADB, Keyguard, and PackageManager
boundaries unchanged.
```

Technology decision:

```text
v0.portal2 uses a token-gated PNG polling stream and root input command bridge:
  GET /api/screen.png -> kp -c "screencap -p"
  POST /api/input -> kp -c "input tap ..." or "input swipe ..."

WebRTC/H.264 is deliberately deferred. The PNG route is simpler to prove on
the current privileged Smartisax service, avoids MediaProjection consent and
libwebrtc/signaling complexity, and gives a fast live gate for the key product
loop: open the phone URL from a LAN browser, see the phone screen, tap, and
drag.
```

Variant:

```text
v0.portal2-smartisax-remote-screen-control
```

Implemented portal additions:

```text

  token-gated PNG response from root screencap

POST /api/input
  token-gated tap/swipe input mapped from browser image coordinates

GET /api/status
  now reports:
    screen=root-screencap-png
    input=root-input-command

server behavior:
  each accepted client socket is handled on a SmartisaxDevicePortalClient
  worker thread so screen polling does not serialize the whole portal server.
```

```text
  MANAGE_DEBUGGING granted=true

```

UI and Portal smoke:

```text
Smartisax UI:
  in-page label now shows SMARTISAX 0.4
  WebView UA reports Chrome/150.0.7871.28
  WebGPU=available
  WebGL2=available
  localStorage ready

Device Portal:
  running=true after tapping Enable with actual 1080x2340 screen coordinates
  URL=http://192.168.31.103:37601
  pairing code=892903
  GET / -> HTTP/1.1 200 OK
  GET /api/status without token -> HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
  POST /api/pair -> token returned
  authorized GET /api/status -> status JSON includes:
    screen=root-screencap-png
    input=root-input-command
```

Failure:

```text
GET /api/screen.png with a valid token returned JSON instead of PNG:
  {"ok":false,"error":"java.io.IOException: Cannot run program \"\/system\/bin\/kp\": error=2, No such file or directory"}

Diagnosis:
  v0.portal2 reused the historical APatch root helper path /system/bin/kp from
  shell/root workflows, but the Smartisax app process cannot see that runtime
  APatch namespace. This matches the earlier Smartisax wireless ADB lesson:
  app-internal kp process calls are not a durable product route.
```

Conclusion:

```text
v0.portal2 is the current live-flashed B-slot ROM and is boot/package/portal
pairing verified, but its screen/input backend is rejected. Continue with a
repair image that replaces app-internal kp calls with privileged Android APIs.
```


```text
boot:
  sys.boot_completed=1
  slot=_b

  package:/system/priv-app/SmartisaxShell/SmartisaxShell.apk
  versionCode=8
  versionName=0.4.1

  MANAGE_DEBUGGING granted=true

verifier result:

```

Permission diagnosis:

```text
android.permission.READ_FRAME_BUFFER:
  protectionLevel: signature
android.permission.CAPTURE_VIDEO_OUTPUT:
  protectionLevel: signature
android.permission.INJECT_EVENTS:
  protectionLevel: signature

Conclusion:
  priv-app + privapp whitelist is insufficient for these signature-only
  platform permissions on this ROM. However, runtime InputManager reflection
  still succeeded in the live smoke below, so the actual blocker for this
  candidate is the screenshot return type conversion, not the input path.
```

Live conclusion:

```text
v0.portal2.1 is flashed and bootable on B slot.
It fixes the v0.portal2 app-internal /system/bin/kp visibility failure and
proves token-gated LAN pairing plus input injection. It does not yet provide a
working screen PNG stream because the app code expected a Bitmap but the live
SurfaceControl call returned a ScreenshotGraphicBuffer-like object.
```

```text
key results:
  GET / -> 200 OK

  POST /api/pair -> token OK

    screen=privileged-surfacecontrol-png
    input=privileged-inputmanager
  GET /api/screen.png -> 500
    {"ok":false,"error":"java.io.IOException: surfacecontrol_screenshot_returned_null"}
  POST /api/input tap -> {"ok":true,"type":"tap","backend":"privileged-inputmanager"}
  POST /api/input swipe -> {"ok":true,"type":"swipe","backend":"privileged-inputmanager"}
  GET /api/screen.png after swipe -> 500
    {"ok":false,"error":"java.io.IOException: surfacecontrol_screenshot_returned_null"}

logcat:
  SurfaceControl: Failed to take screenshot
```

Root/screenshot boundary note:

```text
/system/bin/kp is visible from shell/root namespace:
  adb shell which kp -> /system/bin/kp
  root PATH includes /data/adb/ap/bin

However v0.portal2 already proved Smartisax app-process execution of
/system/bin/kp can return ENOENT, so APatch/shell visibility should not be
treated as proof that zygote-spawned app processes can use that root path.
v0.portal2.2 proves the app-side SurfaceControl path reaches framework code
but framework returns null, likely because READ_FRAME_BUFFER is signature-only
and not granted to the self-signed Smartisax priv-app.
```

Live conclusion:

```text
v0.portal2.2 is flashed and bootable on B slot.
It proves the same-LAN Portal pairing/status contract and direct browser input
control path. It does not yet provide a screen stream. The next repair should
target the screenshot permission/source boundary instead of additional
app-side Bitmap conversion:
  option 1: narrow services.jar/PackageManager policy granting Smartisax only
            the required signature screenshot permission(s)
  option 2: system_server screenshot bridge consumed by Smartisax Portal
```

### 2026-06-22: v0.portal2.3 Smartisax READ_FRAME_BUFFER Narrow Grant Candidate

Purpose:

```text
Build a minimal repair candidate for the v0.portal2.2 screen-stream failure.
The candidate does not change Smartisax APK code. It replaces only
/system/framework/services.jar with a narrow PackageManager signature-permission
policy:

  package: com.smartisax.browser
  permission: android.permission.READ_FRAME_BUFFER

All other signature permissions and packages keep the stock grant path.
```
